name: Emily Randall
subject: Next steps
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
I’m still processing the bittersweet events of this week — winning my election here in Washington while recognizing the defeats we faced nationwide.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96899136/511585412/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.11.10_NextSteps_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNzFjMjBlNS1jMzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KIuu0F5G2SwupfCbaXMcL3AJ9DCXgiiRS_8JJ7bVytI=&emci=2e68d3a6-bb9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=271c20e5-c39f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=23483442) | Peter, I’m still processing the bittersweet events of this week — winning my election here in Washington while recognizing the defeats we faced nationwide. I’ve been fielding calls from constituents over the last few days, including from young LGBTQ+ folks who fear what the future holds from a new Trump administration. [_**As I told King 5 News:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96899137/511585413/-447073117?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.11.10_NextSteps_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNzFjMjBlNS1jMzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KIuu0F5G2SwupfCbaXMcL3AJ9DCXgiiRS_8JJ7bVytI=&emci=2e68d3a6-bb9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=271c20e5-c39f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=23483442) My job in Congress is to deliver results for my neighbors in the 6th District — and just like I did in the State Senate, I’ll work with my new colleagues on both sides of the aisle to do so. **But when it comes to our fundamental freedoms, I will fight with everything I have to preserve and protect our democracy, our ability to control our own bodies, and our freedom to live and love authentically as ourselves.** A few other things I wanted to share: **• Election results:** The people of Washington voted resoundingly for progress this year. Voters elected Bob Ferguson as our next governor, elected Democrats to every executive-level state office, and rejected three out of four right-wing ballot initiatives. And, while votes are still being counted, we expanded our majorities in the State House and Senate. In fact, Washington was one of only two states where Trump gained no ground relative to 2020. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96899138/511585414/183293993?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.11.10_NextSteps_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNzFjMjBlNS1jMzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KIuu0F5G2SwupfCbaXMcL3AJ9DCXgiiRS_8JJ7bVytI=&emci=2e68d3a6-bb9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=271c20e5-c39f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=23483442) **The state of the House:** In Washington, every House Democrat is on track to win reelection, including my friend Marie Gleusenkamp Perez in the competitive 3rd District. But votes are still being counted in several states that will determine which party controls the House of Representatives, particularly in California. In the coming days, I’ll be sharing opportunities to volunteer or donate to support ballot curing and recount efforts in key districts. [_**Vote Save America has compiled several volunteer opportunities here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96899139/511585415/516593337?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2024.11.10_NextSteps_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNzFjMjBlNS1jMzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KIuu0F5G2SwupfCbaXMcL3AJ9DCXgiiRS_8JJ7bVytI=&emci=2e68d3a6-bb9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=271c20e5-c39f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=23483442) I remain incredibly grateful for your support in my campaign. Whatever happens next, we’ll face it together. In service, — Emily

We’ve been working around the clock to ensure every voice in Southern California is heard. This race will come down to just a few votes, so anything we can do to make sure every single vote is counted could make a difference.
We need to deliver the House to ensure we have a fight against the MAGA agenda. But that won’t be possible without flipping CA-45 from red-to-blue.
[All eyes are on CA-45 to see if Democrats can take the House. name: PA Senate Alert
subject: Here’s what you can do today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
Peter, **this Pennsylvania Senate race is not over, and we need your help to re-elect Bob Casey.**
If we’re going to stop Republicans from attacking our fundamental freedoms, we can’t afford to lose another Senate seat.
Bob Casey has fought to protect women’s rights, workers’ rights, and voting rights his entire career, and he won’t stop now.
**The good news is that this race is not over, and votes are still being counted in Pennsylvania.** Our team is working hard to make that happen, but with tens of thousands of votes left to be counted, we need your help.
**[Split a donation of $10 or more between Bob Casey and Lucas Kunce today to ensure we have the resources we need to make sure Pennsylvanians’ voices are heard.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/84110?refcode=em20241110-8660&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8660%2E614464%2EvUp5Z-)**

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Quick poll: Expand early voting?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project Logo [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=111024_1&amount=25]
You are invited to join our one-click issue response poll.
For data integrity purposes, please do not complete this survey if your email address is not dpwillis67@gmail.com .
Early voting was an unqualified success again in 2024. Michael McDonald, a professor of political science at the University of Florida who helps run the school's election lab, told reporters that early voting exploded during the 2020 election and its effectiveness since then has reshaped the way that voters and campaigns navigate elections.
" People find it easier to navigate and return the ballot at their convenience and it gives them more chances. They're more likely to cast a ballot with those options ," he said.
Team: We're looking to hear from our supporters about this way to participation in our elections. Will you take our one-question poll right away?
Are you in favor of expanding early voting availability nationwide?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=111024_1&amount=15] NO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-pollresponse?refcode=111024_1&amount=15]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=111024_1&amount=25]

clean_body: It’s time we held her accountable. | Peter, The American people have spoken. President Trump won back the White House with an overwhelming victory in the Electoral College and won the popular vote by millions of votes! He has a clear mandate, and Americans of all backgrounds should unite to help him implement his America First agenda. But some on the Left will never accept President Trump’s victory... Radical New York Attorney General Tish James has vowed to once again weaponize the law to fight back against President Trump! With President Trump still currently scheduled to be sentenced by a Far Left Democrat judge later this month, we cannot take this threat lightly. As the House GOP Chair and Representative for New York’s 21st Congressional District, I’ve been on the frontlines taking the offensive against the Democrats' LAWFARE for months now. And a critical part of that fight was officially filing a motion to have Tish James DISBARRED over her illegitimate, biased investigation of President Trump. Now that she’s openly threatening President Trump with even more LAWFARE, I’m gathering an additional 25,000,000 signatures from grassroots conservatives for my NATIONAL PETITION in support of my motion to have her DISBARRED. The Left won’t stop fighting to destroy President Trump even after he’s won, so we can’t stop fighting to defend him. It’s time to hold the corrupt left-wing radicals who led these witch hunts accountable. Thank you, Rep. Elise Stefanik House Republican Conference Chair Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC. www.elisestefanik.com PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801 |

body:

Peter,
We’ve done such incredible work together. Today, I’m asking you to keep working with us.

name: Nella Domenici
subject: Thank you, New Mexico!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nella for Senate
body:
| | | | | [](https://nellaforsenate.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6ac79b36261163c3706eb25b8&id=3db74e5562&e=780129cd82)
---
| | | | | **Thank you, New Mexico!**
---
| There is nothing more valuable and valued than your vote at the ballot box. Thank you for entrusting me with yours. Thank you for your support, evidenced in so many ways—in the experiences you shared, the events attended, the coalitions joined, the phone banks manned (and woman-ed), the neighborhoods walked, the financial contributions made, the hugs, smiles and positive energy generously given. And your belief in me--that together we could make New Mexico a better place to live and thrive. I cherish the experiences I’ve had over the last eleven months. It was a year connecting with New Mexicans and discussing the serious challenges we face in this state. What I didn’t expect or fully understand when I entered this race was the depth of the connections, relationships, and friendships I would form with people from all walks of life. I traveled from corner to corner of this state, and met amazing people along the way. This campaign was about people like Kelly Ott, whose son, Austin, died from a fentanyl overdose. This campaign was about people like Carlos and Rita Salazar in Medenales, NM. They worry about access to public lands, their ability to make a living and pay their bills. This campaign was about people like the parents I met in Chaparral, who worry about cartels, drugs and crime flowing across the border and infiltrating their children’s schools. This campaign was about people like Tryna Verbeck, whose husband, Mario, served our community as a police officer and was shot in the neck in a violent shootout in the middle of Albuquerque. This campaign was about the Sergeant from Albuquerque Police Department who I patrolled our most dangerous streets with and who showed me the conditions he and our brave officers work under every single day. This campaign was about schools like Hózhó Academy in Gallup, NM. It’s a charter school that my husband and I helped start with a bipartisan group of New Mexico leaders. Most of Hózhó Academy students are from the Navajo Nation and the school is giving its students the educational foundation needed for success. In the end, this was more than a campaign. It is a movement of people from all walks of life, dedicated to bringing a New Day in New Mexico. My father taught me that the secret to building lifelong friendships and relationships is to work on a project with other people. This eleven-month campaign has been our project for all of us. We are a team locked at the elbows. We have worked together to shine a spotlight on the many problems New Mexico faces. We’re all committed to taking those problems head-on directly and fighting against crime, fighting against our failing schools, and rebuilding our healthcare system. New Mexico is such a rich state. Rich in natural resources, natural beauty, intellectual horsepower, and rich in loving, motivated, kind people of diverse cultures. We need to maintain our commitment to build a better New Mexico, where our grandchildren can live, learn, and thrive, and where our grandparents can get great healthcare and enjoy the sunset years of their lives. And we need to make sure that our state is no longer a destination for criminals, but a wonderful place to raise a family. Writing this, it occurs to me that it will take more than a movement. It will take a crusade for change. Change is necessary to improve New Mexico. Change takes courage and a belief that change is possible. It takes commitment to a vision about the potential and desired result. We need a crusade to” Wake Up New Mexico!” My family and I am so grateful to every one of you who helped us and who believed in me. Thousands of people donated their time, talent, and financial resources because they believed that we can build a New Day in New Mexico.
Thank you, New Mexico.
_**-Nella Domenici**_
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | Thank you, New Mexico! | There is nothing more valuable and valued than your vote at the ballot box. Thank you for entrusting me with yours. Thank you for your support, evidenced in so many ways—in the experiences you shared, the events attended, the coalitions joined, the phone banks manned (and woman-ed), the neighborhoods walked, the financial contributions made, the hugs, smiles and positive energy generously given. And your belief in me--that together we could make New Mexico a better place to live and thrive. I cherish the experiences I’ve had over the last eleven months. It was a year connecting with New Mexicans and discussing the serious challenges we face in this state. What I didn’t expect or fully understand when I entered this race was the depth of the connections, relationships, and friendships I would form with people from all walks of life. I traveled from corner to corner of this state, and met amazing people along the way. This campaign was about people like Kelly Ott, whose son, Austin, died from a fentanyl overdose. This campaign was about people like Carlos and Rita Salazar in Medenales, NM. They worry about access to public lands, their ability to make a living and pay their bills. This campaign was about people like the parents I met in Chaparral, who worry about cartels, drugs and crime flowing across the border and infiltrating their children’s schools. This campaign was about people like Tryna Verbeck, whose husband, Mario, served our community as a police officer and was shot in the neck in a violent shootout in the middle of Albuquerque. This campaign was about the Sergeant from Albuquerque Police Department who I patrolled our most dangerous streets with and who showed me the conditions he and our brave officers work under every single day. This campaign was about schools like Hózhó Academy in Gallup, NM. It’s a charter school that my husband and I helped start with a bipartisan group of New Mexico leaders. Most of Hózhó Academy students are from the Navajo Nation and the school is giving its students the educational foundation needed for success. In the end, this was more than a campaign. It is a movement of people from all walks of life, dedicated to bringing a New Day in New Mexico. My father taught me that the secret to building lifelong friendships and relationships is to work on a project with other people. This eleven-month campaign has been our project for all of us. We are a team locked at the elbows. We have worked together to shine a spotlight on the many problems New Mexico faces. We’re all committed to taking those problems head-on directly and fighting against crime, fighting against our failing schools, and rebuilding our healthcare system. New Mexico is such a rich state. Rich in natural resources, natural beauty, intellectual horsepower, and rich in loving, motivated, kind people of diverse cultures. We need to maintain our commitment to build a better New Mexico, where our grandchildren can live, learn, and thrive, and where our grandparents can get great healthcare and enjoy the sunset years of their lives. And we need to make sure that our state is no longer a destination for criminals, but a wonderful place to raise a family. Writing this, it occurs to me that it will take more than a movement. It will take a crusade for change. Change is necessary to improve New Mexico. Change takes courage and a belief that change is possible. It takes commitment to a vision about the potential and desired result. We need a crusade to” Wake Up New Mexico!” My family and I am so grateful to every one of you who helped us and who believed in me. Thousands of people donated their time, talent, and financial resources because they believed that we can build a New Day in New Mexico. Thank you, New Mexico. -Nella Domenici | | | | | | [Paid for by Nella for Senate.]() | Copyright (C) 2024 NELLA FOR SENATE All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: P.O. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 11/10/2024
body:
Did Colorado turn a bit more “pink” this election?
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**November 10, 2024** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Friends,
This election season brought some bright spots for Colorado conservatives and underscored how much our collective efforts matter in protecting Colorado’s influence and values.
**This week’s results show that Colorado is a lot closer to “pink” than a blue island in a sea of red as the liberal media said.** Several races tipped in our favor. We flipped Pueblo County, a region that Biden barely held in 2020, as it shifted from blue to red with Trump winning by 2.47 points. Pitkin County (Aspen) went 7 points to the right!
We also saw a major victory in Colorado’s 8th Congressional District, with [Gabe Evans unseating Yadira Caraveo](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). We held [Congressional District 3](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) with Jeff Hurd, Congressional District 4 with Lauren Boebert and Congressional District 5 with Jeff Crank.
On top of that, efforts to [hold the line on the Democrat supermajority](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) look to be paying off (we’ll see this week). These gains reaffirm that our message and values resonate, especially in key areas where voters turned out to make an impact.
Other victories this election cycle include flipping a seat on the State Board of Education with [Yazmin Navarro’s win](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), preserving a pro-charter stance for Colorado’s students and parents. Additionally, Colorado voters stopped both [ranked-choice voting and a measure to ban mountain lion hunting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), showing that, even in a purple state, commonsense values still have strong support.
Lastly, this election has showcased increasing support for conservative ideas among diverse groups nationwide. Trump improved his results from 2020 but also gained traction across various demographics, including voters under 30, Hispanic voters, and even reliably blue areas like some of our resort counties. It’s clear that as we promote policies grounded in common sense, the movement grows stronger, broader, and more resilient.
Let’s take these wins as fuel to continue advocating for Colorado’s values. With your support and engagement, Rocky Mountain Voice will keep providing the information and stories that matter most to you and to the future of our state.
In liberty, Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[‘Unprecedented’ mistake by Secretary Griswold calls for unprecedented ruling, Libertarian Party argues in District Court](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Some daylight may have been breathed Monday afternoon into what some have called the largest coverup in the history of Colorado’s mail-in balloting era, and perhaps ever in its secretary of state’s office.

**[Long after ‘the call’ there’s work being done in election offices across Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When the polls close and enough ballots are counted on Election Night, voters are familiar with news agencies “calling” a winner in a race.
**[Schumann: It’s a party in MAGA Country, as Trump’s return to White House signals a mandate for change](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When Americans awoke Nov. 6, following an Election Night which went into the wee hours of the early morning, they learned Donald J. Trump had been elected as the 47th U.S. President with 286 electoral votes, defeating Kamala Harris.

|
---
**STATE**
**[Gabe Evans unseats Yadira Caraveo in Colorado’s 8th Congressional District](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Republican state Rep. Gabe Evans has unseated Democratic U.S. Rep. Yadira Caraveo in Colorado’s highly competitive 8th Congressional District, a win that represents the biggest victory for the GOP in Colorado since 2014.

**[Some candidates still watching and waiting to see if final returns can flip their fortunes](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Polls closed nearly 72 hours ago at this point, but there are still some races in Colorado that could drag into the weekend and perhaps next week before a clear winner is decided.
**[Pugliese elected House minority leader, Winter as assistant leader by Republicans](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Rep. Rose Pugliese will again serve as Colorado House Minority Leader, following an unopposed election to the position Friday, Nov. 8. She will be joined in Republican House leadership by Assistant Minority Leader Ty Winter, Minority Whip Ryan Armagost and Caucus Chair Anthony Hartsook.

**[How did Colorado vote? Here’s a quick rundown on 2024 statewide ballot initiatives](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) An estimated 63.2% of Coloradans — more than 2.5 million — turned out via mail-in, drop-off or in-person voting to help decide a variety of items on one of the longest ballots in memory. For them to decide were seven Colorado constitutional amendments and seven more state propositions.
**[District court denies Libertarian Party’s plea for a hand-count of ballots in Colorado following password leak](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver District Court Judge Kandace Gerdes has denied an appeal to seek a hand-count of election returns and other relief sought by the Libertarian Party in a suit against Secretary of State Jena Griswold and the State of Colorado.
_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Tim Scott launches bid to chair NRSC as GOP seeks to capitalize on new minority gains](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., is running to lead the Senate Republican campaign arm in the next election cycle, sharing the announcement exclusively with Fox News Digital.

**[Trump announces Susie Wiles as Chief of Staff](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President-elect Donald Trump announced Thursday that his 2024 campaign manager, Susie Wiles will be his Chief of Staff in his administration.
**[Across America on Election Night, voters mostly rejected ranked-choice voting measures](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Voters across the country weighed in on ranked choice voting ballot initiatives Tuesday, with a vast majority rejecting the system amid an intensifying debate over its effects on elections.
**[When was the last time the Republican Party won the popular vote?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE HILL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Former President Donald Trump has won the presidency over Vice President Kamala Harris, winning both the electoral college vote and the popular vote, something that has become very rare for a Republican candidate to do since the turn of the century.

**[Unrest on the horizon: Coordinated anti-establishment protests planned across U.S. cities](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Cities across the United States are preparing for a wave of protests and potential unrest, as various anti-establishment groups signal their intent to take to the streets regardless of the election outcome.

**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[In Grand Junction, City Council debates role in homeless solution, problem shelter](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) After months of appealing to the Grand Junction City Council to do something about the criminal element that has worsened since the Homeless Day Center was located in their neighborhood, business owners and downtown residents are beginning to see progress.
**[On Denver’s mile long ballot, here’s what passed and what didn’t](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver voters approved the majority of the dozen initiatives on this year’s ballot. They turned down a few. Read more for a look at what passed and what didn’t on the Denver ballot.
**[Denver voters reject Mike Johnston’s sales tax hike for affordable housing, latest tally shows](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver voters have rejected Mayor Mike Johnston’s proposal to increase the city’s sales tax rate by 0.5 points in order to pay for housing projects citywide.
**[Voters approve lodging taxes for child care in Montrose, along with La Plata, Grand counties](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [CHALKBEAT COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Voters in three western Colorado communities approved lodging tax measures in Tuesday’s election that will send money to efforts aimed at making child care more affordable and easier to find.
**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Gilmartin: Win in House District 50 by Ryan Gonzalez, age 29, is historic](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In a groundbreaking election for Colorado Republicans, 29-year-old Ryan Gonzalez claimed victory in House District 50 in Greeley, unseating incumbent Democrat Mary Young._
**[Kalam: Lessons (not) learned from the 2024 election](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _As I settled into my usual spot at my go-to Colfax bar for its election watch party, donning my black MAGA hat, I could feel the tension even before the manager approached me_
**[Analysis: Harris dominated in seven Front Range Colorado counties, deciding election](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _As middle America largely was a sea of Red on Tuesday evening and into the morning, Colorado was a liberal outlier, going with Kamala Harris for President._ __
**[Williams: In effort to Make America Healthy Again, let’s remove government regulations on raw milk](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Do you remember when the stereotype of a “crunchy granola liberal” was an insult so partisan that no conservative dared even to step into a health food store?_
**[Schumann: Griswold’s apology for election security breach draws comparisons to Tina Peters’ case](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _In a recent turn of events, Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold is now expressing regret over the actions coming from her office._
_**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---

|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** Only 13 U.S. presidents have been **elected and served two full terms**. Franklin D. Roosevelt holds the record as the longest-serving president, elected to four terms before the Twenty-second Amendment limited presidents to two terms.

clean_body: Did Colorado turn a bit more “pink” this election? | | | | November 10, 2024 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Friends, This election season brought some bright spots for Colorado conservatives and underscored how much our collective efforts matter in protecting Colorado’s influence and values. This week’s results show that Colorado is a lot closer to “pink” than a blue island in a sea of red as the liberal media said. Several races tipped in our favor. We flipped Pueblo County, a region that Biden barely held in 2020, as it shifted from blue to red with Trump winning by 2.47 points. Pitkin County (Aspen) went 7 points to the right! We also saw a major victory in Colorado’s 8th Congressional District, with . We held with Jeff Hurd, Congressional District 4 with Lauren Boebert and Congressional District 5 with Jeff Crank. On top of that, efforts to look to be paying off (we’ll see this week). These gains reaffirm that our message and values resonate, especially in key areas where voters turned out to make an impact. Other victories this election cycle include flipping a seat on the State Board of Education with , preserving a pro-charter stance for Colorado’s students and parents. Additionally, Colorado voters stopped both , showing that, even in a purple state, commonsense values still have strong support. Lastly, this election has showcased increasing support for conservative ideas among diverse groups nationwide. Trump improved his results from 2020 but also gained traction across various demographics, including voters under 30, Hispanic voters, and even reliably blue areas like some of our resort counties. It’s clear that as we promote policies grounded in common sense, the movement grows stronger, broader, and more resilient. Let’s take these wins as fuel to continue advocating for Colorado’s values. With your support and engagement, Rocky Mountain Voice will keep providing the information and stories that matter most to you and to the future of our state. In liberty, Heidi Ganahl | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK Some daylight may have been breathed Monday afternoon into what some have called the largest coverup in the history of Colorado’s mail-in balloting era, and perhaps ever in its secretary of state’s office. When the polls close and enough ballots are counted on Election Night, voters are familiar with news agencies “calling” a winner in a race. When Americans awoke Nov. 6, following an Election Night which went into the wee hours of the early morning, they learned Donald J. Trump had been elected as the 47th U.S. President with 286 electoral votes, defeating Kamala Harris. | STATE Republican state Rep. Gabe Evans has unseated Democratic U.S. Rep. Yadira Caraveo in Colorado’s highly competitive 8th Congressional District, a win that represents the biggest victory for the GOP in Colorado since 2014. Polls closed nearly 72 hours ago at this point, but there are still some races in Colorado that could drag into the weekend and perhaps next week before a clear winner is decided. Rep. Rose Pugliese will again serve as Colorado House Minority Leader, following an unopposed election to the position Friday, Nov. 8. She will be joined in Republican House leadership by Assistant Minority Leader Ty Winter, Minority Whip Ryan Armagost and Caucus Chair Anthony Hartsook. An estimated 63.2% of Coloradans — more than 2.5 million — turned out via mail-in, drop-off or in-person voting to help decide a variety of items on one of the longest ballots in memory. For them to decide were seven Colorado constitutional amendments and seven more state propositions. Denver District Court Judge Kandace Gerdes has denied an appeal to seek a hand-count of election returns and other relief sought by the Libertarian Party in a suit against Secretary of State Jena Griswold and the State of Colorado. | NATIONAL Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., is running to lead the Senate Republican campaign arm in the next election cycle, sharing the announcement exclusively with Fox News Digital. President-elect Donald Trump announced Thursday that his 2024 campaign manager, Susie Wiles will be his Chief of Staff in his administration. Voters across the country weighed in on ranked choice voting ballot initiatives Tuesday, with a vast majority rejecting the system amid an intensifying debate over its effects on elections. Former President Donald Trump has won the presidency over Vice President Kamala Harris, winning both the electoral college vote and the popular vote, something that has become very rare for a Republican candidate to do since the turn of the century. Cities across the United States are preparing for a wave of protests and potential unrest, as various anti-establishment groups signal their intent to take to the streets regardless of the election outcome. | LOCAL After months of appealing to the Grand Junction City Council to do something about the criminal element that has worsened since the Homeless Day Center was located in their neighborhood, business owners and downtown residents are beginning to see progress. Denver voters approved the majority of the dozen initiatives on this year’s ballot. They turned down a few. Read more for a look at what passed and what didn’t on the Denver ballot. Denver voters have rejected Mayor Mike Johnston’s proposal to increase the city’s sales tax rate by 0.5 points in order to pay for housing projects citywide. Voters in three western Colorado communities approved lodging tax measures in Tuesday’s election that will send money to efforts aimed at making child care more affordable and easier to find. | COMMENTARY In a groundbreaking election for Colorado Republicans, 29-year-old Ryan Gonzalez claimed victory in House District 50 in Greeley, unseating incumbent Democrat Mary Young. As I settled into my usual spot at my go-to Colfax bar for its election watch party, donning my black MAGA hat, I could feel the tension even before the manager approached me As middle America largely was a sea of Red on Tuesday evening and into the morning, Colorado was a liberal outlier, going with Kamala Harris for President. Do you remember when the stereotype of a “crunchy granola liberal” was an insult so partisan that no conservative dared even to step into a health food store? In a recent turn of events, Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold is now expressing regret over the actions coming from her office. _ | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? # A SPECIAL ELECTION WILL BE HELD
# IN EARLY DECEMBER TO FILL JOHN MCGUIRE'S SEAT
---
| Dear Dear Did you know there will be a Special Election to be held in early December of THIS year to fill the Virginia Senate Seat in District 10 to be vacated by John McGuire, a current Virginia Senate and Republican nominee for Congress in District 5?
The election will take place just 5 days after Senator Louise Lucas calls for a Special Election. So you will have less than 5 days to hear of the election and know to cast your vote.
The election will take place quickly and I want to make sure it's on your radar. The election will take place right after this coming Thanksgiving but before Christmas. So please be alert during the holidays this year.
As an experienced Virginia Senator who served in the Senate for 8 years, I am the only candidate running who has the experience to hit the ground running on day 1.
Senate District 10 is also one of the most conservative districts in Virginia. When I served in the Senate from from 2016 to 2023, I was considered one of the most conservative Virginia Senators in Virginia by various conservative watchdog groups like the Family Foundation, the Virginia Citizen's Defense League, the National Rifle Association and the National Federation of Independent Businesses- all 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate. It's hard to beat a rating of 100%.
I'm the only candidate running with the knowledge, experience and capability to be effective, day one.
Please listen to my interview with both Joe Thomas and John Reid. I'm all in!
If you have any questions, please feel free to hit reply and I will personally respond to your message.
---
|
---

Many generations of our family have served in our military dating all the way back to the beginning in the Revolutionary War. In this old photo with my brother and father, both Army, I was assigned to SEAL Team Four in the Navy. My brother Rusty McGuire is now a Brigadier General in the Virginia National Guard and my oldest son John now serves in the Navy.

clean_body: McGuire achives huge VA05 Vote Record | | | | == | == | Happy Veterans Day! Happy Veterans Day! We would not have a country without our men and women who served in our military. Thank you for your service and your sacrifice! == | | | | ---|--- Many generations of our family have served in our military dating all the way back to the beginning in the Revolutionary War. In this old photo with my brother and father, both Army, I was assigned to SEAL Team Four in the Navy. My brother Rusty McGuire is now a Brigadier General in the Virginia National Guard and my oldest son John now serves in the Navy. For a Veterans Day story of Redemption == | Thanks to you I received more votes than anyone in history for the 5th Congressional District. Thank you President Trump, Ramona Christian, our volunteers, and so many more for helping us get out our message! It will be an honor to continue service to you as your Congressman for Virginia's 5th Congressional District. # A SPECIAL ELECTION WILL BE HELD
# IN EARLY DECEMBER TO FILL JOHN MCGUIRE'S SEAT
---
| Dear Dear Did you know there will be a Special Election to be held in early December of THIS year to fill the Virginia Senate Seat in District 10 to be vacated by John McGuire, a current Virginia Senate and Republican nominee for Congress in District 5?
The election will take place just 5 days after Senator Louise Lucas calls for a Special Election. So you will have less than 5 days to hear of the election and know how you can cast your vote.
The election will take place quickly and I want to make sure it's on your radar. The election will take place right after this coming Thanksgiving but before Christmas. So please be alert during the holidays this year.
As an experienced Virginia Senator who served in the Senate for 8 years, I am the only candidate running who has the experience to hit the ground running on day 1.
Senate District 10 is also one of the most conservative districts in Virginia. When I served in the Senate from from 2016 to 2023, I was considered one of the most conservative Virginia Senators in Virginia by various conservative watchdog groups like the Family Foundation, the Virginia Citizen's Defense League, the National Rifle Association and the National Federation of Independent Businesses- all 8 years I served in the Virginia Senate. It's hard to beat a rating of 100%.
I'm the only candidate running with the knowledge, experience and capability to be effective, day one.
Please listen to my recent press interviews with both Joe Thomas and John Reid. And tune in to John Frederick. I'm all in!
If you have any questions, please feel free to hit reply and I will personally respond to your message.
---
|
---
Senator Amanda Chase will:
* Keep our District Rural
* And Our Economy Strong.

Amanda is:
* Proven Conservative
* Battle tested
* Experienced
* Fully Committed
* Strong
* Ready Day One!

* Pro-God
* Pro-Trump
* Pro-Life
* Pro-2A
* Pro-Constitution
* Pro-TRUE Election Integrity
* Pro-School Choice
* Pro-Law Enforcement
* Pro-Veteran
* Pro-Small Business
* Pro-Liberty

I wanted to wish all of our Veterans a happy Veterans Day. https://click.updates.troydowning.com/?qs=e0f029d896cef876129ef93791e34613263d69048531950ff2e4cc2a1bd1e13727d6fc3eea62c22d264430f4a8731f898e05617cba973e3cfff63a5106cfea10 To every man and woman who has worn the uniform of this great nation, your courage, sacrifice, and unwavering dedication to this country will never be forgotten. You embody the strength and resolve of these United States, and it is because of you that we remain the land of the free. As a fellow Veteran, I know the commitment it takes to serve, and I’m honored to have served alongside incredible Americans. When I get to Congress, I pledge to fight with vigor, strength, and integrity to ensure every Veteran receives the respect, care, and support they’ve earned. Today, I ask you to join me in reflecting on the service and sacrifice of Veterans and, just as important, their families. Let’s show them the gratitude they deserve—today and every day. name: Sign the Petition (via Left Action)
subject: Sign: Demand Senate votes for Trump appointments
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Trump must get Senate approval for officials he appoints. Don't let him skip this Constitutional requirement](https://default.salsalabs.org/T08daf2c9-3337-49a7-b630-2a326829de6e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7d01e211-d23b-4f4a-952f-7de0dc367fde/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Presidential appointees for cabinet and other key positions must get Senate approval. This has long been the case, and it's part of the Senate's job to offer "advice and consent" that is written into the Constitution. **[But Trump is demanding that Republican leaders let him appoint whoever he wants, without a vote.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf6e64c94-675e-4d61-a4ae-83d0b0dcd448/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) This would let him unilaterally appoint the most extreme lunatics possible, without Senate approval. And it would let Senators avoid having to cast tough votes.** There is no way in hell the Senate should allow this to happen. Trump cannot be allowed to appoint unqualified and dangerous individuals to critically important positions. [**Tell the Senate to do its job. Demand Trump get Senate approval for his appointments.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3cb5dadd-202c-4322-8d4d-51a68954eace/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

## U.S., Mexico Amend Water Treaty
The U.S. and Mexico have agreed to [amend a 1944 water treaty](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGuqzAQRL_GdEZrryFJ4eI1dHntLZHBa7BkcGRvQvL3V6G_U49mjs7Pfby7mOK-_HcbWX6HPGrQRiollRq9i-kzmsbbCfxNNWTVBRVqc-1vzWoBeiQC8MFcMGgFuguTJt-ZGbE3XRPtd0ydQY2qhRmu5Du8TQGmHowwQD5yLi2vxPR2NaRPflFp57w1ya7Mjyrwn9CD0MNxHG1187MU2vnbEHrY6ahCD88qN3rHOcvDMRXplkK00c5yi8vKcipxX2ShFClIzvLhyrySlzU_eZXns8QeEI3ummL944gpxdpfhIFlczGdQPynoZfVvwEAAP__OXhphA) to address shortages threatening South Texas farmers. Under the treaty, Mexico is required to [deliver 1.75 million acre-feet of water](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDmO6zAQRE8jZTS4aQsY_ESZfzqh0DZbdANcjCbl5fYDO5-KqwoP7-e8nYEi5fAfErr22sumpbZCKaHU5oHie7O9dxfpF9WjU5NRRtt5XPqbs3ZQ3s8wmHkazT54nLUBmOdxnKRddE_uc6a-Mdqok7zKGf1glssuL6O0nZXoqRU-tRs2fEHd47s8kE_Xkvrobq3da2f-dXrt9MpUAkP2GA5gT5A_rU6vlBtyhkYlQxQQGDFhbqJSyOhFKwJzPRhFKoyCMRwRWDyhIQuPkR7IhFXsXJJI-KJr-WwOUTu99uz8_UkxUh2nzsqQgOKXrv2p6-H0bwAAAP__cdFwAw) to the United States every five years, but they currently owe 1.3 million acre-feet with less than a year remaining before the October 2025 deadline. The agreement permits Mexico to relinquish its allotment of water, transfer water [stored at the Falcon and Amistad reservoirs](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L9uwyAYBPCngQ2Lf7HNwNDFW7p2tDB8TpBwcOFLiN--cvbe_NOddD_X-epiio_bt9vA4nvNs-RSMyGYEHNwMR2zpsEuPBhBwYpBCSX12Bt6t6D7lY_OhMWMDrg3_SAVX1XvB3ER3tBozzLxiZJKdNzzEcJFmWXlS8810RxCxFw6vAPC29U1HfkFpfN5o8neEfdK1BeRE5FTa617uZTg8PDA4tKpiJyWXAIUVmDPBYmcNnhHn9le8p4rVPZ8xN8nsOYOhvlk7mDNIRQWkeV2ikrkRIsNe4spxdoPRPPb5uJnguK_z7ys_AsAAP__mM5oOw) along the Rio Grande, and supply water from the San Juan and Alamo rivers, which don’t drain into the Rio Grande. The amendment was initially scheduled to take effect in December 2023, but Mexican officials said they wouldn’t agree to it until after their June presidential elections. Unless it's extended, the amendment will remain in effect for five years.
## City Agrees To Mediation Over Proposed Church
The North Texas town of Fairview and the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints will [enter mediation over a proposed temple](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0UL2OszAQfBrTLfLfR6BwkYbmU6456a5EBi9hT-ZH9hKStz-R_qaZGY00Gs33rbt5irTcP_yMjp_j2mmpLSgFSnXBU3x1tgiul6FRBTp1McpoW1dNMblaY2N61KZRwXtl0DZW-1pWuqkrM-qC3Fmm3jDaqFIOssbwzzT9KPtKWmElBuI1lTwh49PnMb7WB6ZyWOciuol5y8JchW6Fbo_jKOe8nJnQLS6wZ6HbBY-T3nr0lB6EBwzTnoYJ1hF-MO8ZhilR5tNHz4wJgn9B9rRwhgl9AF5hxkCeaV2AFmCct4gQKG87o9CtT3C9Kv7_9ZmK5MJ2UIyUq4uw8j57iu_F_OeFD6d_AwAA__9xynUp) that would exceed local zoning height limits. In August, the city council [denied the church a building permit](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOgzAQRL_GdKD1QhwoXFzjLtdeiWx2naxkAsK-OPn7U9LflDOaGen9XOaLlyT367df2ZZn3GYEHFqtW61n8pJe89CQDUCTbtjqc697HEYzNTfrIVCME5joDY10xkWPTP4EiyGYMDRi32P6ox573cECI9Opn0KEYGBQAzBJ2Y6u3Ljw0-eYXtuDj27Z1ibZWyl7Vv2XQqfQ1Vq7JeQ71_zOFbpPRaF7Wwpd9HI8hGu7Mokvst3bRLktvO6JW5K8_xZW6JrD0l4lJcnmrAa4rl7S57L8y-Bh8S8AAP__KHhgPQ) for a 65-foot temple with a spire nearly 174 feet tall, citing a 35-foot height limit for residential areas and opposition from residents who felt the imposing structure would detract from the community’s small-town feel. Last month, the city [created a legal defense fund](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0ULtu5DAQ-xq5G0Ov2LuFim3c5cpcacjSKJ6D_IA0Xmf__uDtw4YkCBAE_36On54yrd9__IKOf9I2aqktKAVKjdFTfo22iW6S8a4adKo3ymh76-7N7KyNH702vUF9s16GEJKRMaGeOuy8TA25q0y9YbRRrQzyhvHD3Kckp05aYSVG4q20PCPjj68pv7YnljZsS5PdzLxXYR5CD0IP53m2S12vTOgBVziq0MOK50VvnTyVJ-EJFRiXPSNEqvvBCCfxDGE-SphhS_AP61EhzIUqXz57ZiwQ_Quqp5UreGBaMNOKQg--wOOhOKqvr6a4uJ-UM9WuF1Z-L57yey7_-t_T6f8BAAD___VadMc) and invited donations, anticipating a potential lawsuit from the church, which asserts that its right to religious expression should supersede concerns about aesthetic appearances. Former U.S. Magistrate Jeff Kaplan will oversee the mediation scheduled for Nov. 18.
## Bull Trainer Won't Give Up
With the help of his friends and family, Austin Patterson has refused to give up after [suffering life-threatening injuries](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uhDAQhJ_GdEb2mvBTuEhDd2lTImMvsIrBJ7N3HG8fcamz1Urf6NNovm_DzVGkbf5yK1p-TWkABZXUWmo9BEfxHKoi2FGFThdodWO0gaqtu2KxoVWNbzr0ME2h7szUae_BtAF8XXvsCrKXTL_PgNGl8qrF8GG6cVJjrSpRKQzEKZe8IOPL7VM80xNz6dNaRLsw33dhPgX0AvrjOMqfg18XFNBfbgG91gJ61V4cM8qMLpxywXiXC60yp4BJ-rSuj434vD7cJacZecH8l0sbSl6QskzHJt3EmOX4iFFmCrTN0nlPATcW0BfZhvtBMdJeN6JS8-oovsvyv-s9LfwGAAD__0M2cys) at a recent rodeo. Although a neck injury in 2019 ended his bull-riding career, Patterson remained active in the community by raising and training bulls. During a recent event, he intervened to restrain a bull but was severely injured, suffering broken ribs, a broken sternum and pelvis, a collapsed lung, and a lacerated liver. Patterson has shown remarkable recovery progress and [looks forward to his upcoming wedding](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7-OhCAYBPCngQ7Dv8O1oLjGbq-90iB87JKgGPhc17e_uP1NPflN5vc-3V3KaX38uAUsvmOZJJeaCcGEmIJL-Zw0DXbmYRAUrOiVUFLfzECftjfG-VkNveKDE0pJrSByI-DmZXQ80GQvTHyipBId9_wG4UsNc-Sz4ZpoDiFhqR0-AeHtWsxneUHtfFlotk_ErRH1TeRI5HgcR_cocV_DAleByDESObZ920pF5vaGaW2sgr-Ik7mIUNm858zS6lOAFWm1YTtSzqmZnmj-WFzKnzH89_3Lyr8AAAD__zpcXeI). To help with medical bills, a friend held a benefit auction on Facebook, raising over $60,000.

Abilene 🌤️ 74/43° | Amarillo ☁️ 70/39° | Austin ☀️ 83/55° | Brownsville 🌥️ 87/69° | Corpus Christi 🌦️ 87/69° | DFW ⛅ 76/52° | El Paso 🌥️ 76/42° | Houston 🌤️ 82/67° | Lubbock ☁️ 73/40° | San Antonio 🌤️ 85/62° | Texarkana 🌤️ 75/53° | U.S., Mexico Amend Water Treaty The U.S. and Mexico have agreed to to address shortages threatening South Texas farmers. Under the treaty, Mexico is required to to the United States every five years, but they currently owe 1.3 million acre-feet with less than a year remaining before the October 2025 deadline. The agreement permits Mexico to relinquish its allotment of water, transfer water along the Rio Grande, and supply water from the San Juan and Alamo rivers, which don’t drain into the Rio Grande. The amendment was initially scheduled to take effect in December 2023, but Mexican officials said they wouldn’t agree to it until after their June presidential elections. Unless it's extended, the amendment will remain in effect for five years. City Agrees To Mediation Over Proposed Church The North Texas town of Fairview and the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints will that would exceed local zoning height limits. In August, the city council for a 65-foot temple with a spire nearly 174 feet tall, citing a 35-foot height limit for residential areas and opposition from residents who felt the imposing structure would detract from the community’s small-town feel. Last month, the city and invited donations, anticipating a potential lawsuit from the church, which asserts that its right to religious expression should supersede concerns about aesthetic appearances. Former U.S. Magistrate Jeff Kaplan will oversee the mediation scheduled for Nov. 18. Bull Trainer Won't Give Up With the help of his friends and family, Austin Patterson has refused to give up after at a recent rodeo. Although a neck injury in 2019 ended his bull-riding career, Patterson remained active in the community by raising and training bulls. During a recent event, he intervened to restrain a bull but was severely injured, suffering broken ribs, a broken sternum and pelvis, a collapsed lung, and a lacerated liver. Patterson has shown remarkable recovery progress and . To help with medical bills, a friend held a benefit auction on Facebook, raising over $60,000. # Monday, November 11, 2024
---|---
**Good Morning**! On this day in 1918, at the eleventh hour, the Armistice was signed, ending World War I. A year later, [Armistice Day](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7FuwyAQxvGngc2W70CYDAxdvKVrR4vAEa7CIcIolt--SvZ-86ef9P-5rlfPhR_3b7-R66muOKEeAAaANXou56pldIkISUlyMCtQqK2dZHbB4IyBbjFZrRJYNMEnpS0YT4nISHZvDT5TWtnxEimgBaMuN2-sTUJPFLnXNv5WfvRMqZz1RW0MdZPF5d6fu1BfAheBy3EcY-a913Zm7u-LwMW3jffOgYboT4GLbC4-Dy6FdzMLPd03z-XD9f_yXg7_AgAA__-GIlJI) was established, now observed in the U.S. as Veterans Day. We're excited to announce that we'll be launching our newest state publication, _**The Colorado Flyover,** _on Tuesday._ _ Readers interested in daily news covering the Centennial state can click here to [sign up automatically. ](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFOwzAQQL_G3hrZd47jDh4QURZUGJFYIhOfE4MTV4kp6t-jAEMHuO2d3rvhnk_9ycUUl_HRzWRLyD0IUAcpD1L23sV07RX3NhABIScrG5QIyhjBJ0syeIc1Cgp1kBqgGcDrOqBxYgce7X5Nfg8qNNXR0wBGajy-Om1MYEqQjyWv1VuOS5kopGu-0FoNeebJTqWcN4Z3DDoG3Zz-sBh0v7QbboxDiss7w45mFxPD1p8_Y0px0w2DWolxX_-EeiuuUD9kTwzb-ycG-qPMDNuHF77am-6m4uW_J10sfAUAAP__7V5ovQ) Today, we're flying with our sponsor, [Frontieras North America](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO8yAQBOCnwV0sWPgxLih-KXKXa6-0sFnsPQG2MMkpb39yirsq2-6nkWY-b-PNUaS8fLiEtoZtBA7qIsRFiNE7is9RNd4GREDZoBWdFBKUMbxZ7axnHoRGE4TwXcAOhOodTOD8pNBPDdkzTbxOKmna3uMMRmjZT04bE5ji6Klupf3aKNcVQ3xuDyztvKUm2rXW_WDyP4OBwUD5gUdtQ9lyJSzuONX5ksO9pvHY7mVGJq8-lT8TXfaUl90tyECfLqGne2LyurtSM5ZjpV0YfpH_5PjqzUDX7BIeTF6H36B3ehSd1D3vegDeFOv3b4qRDt0xxZfkKL661HfbPiz8BAAA__8kOHu9), which offers an opportunity to invest in an untapped $2.1 trillion energy market.
|

## Russia, Ukraine Exchange Major Drone Strikes
Russia and Ukraine [traded drone strikes](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O6yAUhJ_G7oj4OTZQUNwmXW67pYXhkJxdbCIgsfL2q6Tf6UYjjT59X5fl4inTfv3vN3Q9lUVyCUwIJsQSPeXXAmN0CVGiGtEJrYSSYAwfbw6VXScjlDazWY2xk7LC6El4GWergI_k3m_iEwXKnGzEII2YlV39bEwagGOkXurpu9Deb5jyqzyxnkLZxuxuvd_boP4N8jzIs7_veLT39C61U8g4yHN9tEaePX6qpx3Z4SvbSgvlYLGWHRuTHFKEmEAHSB5WnDiASBPawEEHNVYX7wflTG3WA_Dr5il_CPpfRp5O_gYAAP__-QRgmw) overnight Saturday, with Ukraine’s attack hitting Moscow, resulting in several injuries and airport disruptions. The attacks mark the [largest drone strikes](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GOgzAQBNCvsbsg79oxpnBxDV2uvRIt9hIcGRyBFS5_fyL9TTXSSE-an9two5TTev-mhX2dyoAKzQXgAjBESvk9GBn9xIysJXtoNWg0zik5ezu6EOLYUrxGTQydsdeJyCnrsOMryeRPDT7RRrumixzQgdXdSNa5SRjFMdWyNY-S1jrzlN_lxVsTyiKzn2t97kJ_CewF9sdxNOMYzk1gv_KxC-xpqylkPmv4RfeICsBsRW4-Po-Uc9ptK4y6L5TyR63_vXx5_AsAAP__dD9TVA) since the war began. The escalation comes directly after Russian President Putin signed an alliance with North Korea into law and amid reports that he is considering President-elect [Donald Trump’s proposals](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO8jAQhJ8m6YyyaxPbhYu_oeNvr4wW7xp8Z2LkGBBvf4L-phuNNPr0fR2XI-WS1_N_ukroqS44oVEACmBhyuW1mJFDEkHRowSwGjQa56bxEmifhPdMSbNYx2AhzRMJOknowU9jDu83-EQb7XaeJaKDWfsTzc6lwUzCude2-6557RdJ5VUf0naxXscSLr3ftkH_G_Aw4IFuqzy39_QuredYZMBDu29bJnX_aZRXUU9qqrJspE61NSlFcaurKIKZT9qmRHYfdYwe4wQ0i2frwWAaW-DbM5eSt9kOZjpfKZcPR__LyyPgbwAAAP__KFVkuQ) for ending the conflict. Meanwhile, a top official in the U.K. said the Russian troops had suffered their [worst month of casualties](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFvwyAUhH8N3myZBwUyMHTxlq4dLQLP8WswREDi5t9Xzt6bTrrTnb7v83x2FCldv9yGti15hhFkz3nP-Rwcxdcsu2AXREDRoeVacAHSmLFbrVT6Q0mUQYPRxgsvDSgNqJVEt-hTR_ZY428JKcxwCujBcCVOF6eMWZgcMVDLZfjJlNqKS3zlJ5bB562Ldm3tXpn4ZDAxmPZ9H_ylJtzrkTOYDstgKo9ayfWPW3GUsMdfv7p0xT6UnLCvrdANj15XbLjvFCNVpZkcr5uj-L5q_6E_LfwFAAD__2N4W3k) in October since they invaded Ukraine in February 2022, estimating a total of 700,000 Russian casualties in the war.
## Ruling on Trump Conviction Coming Tuesday
New York judge Juan Merchan is [set to decide on Tuesday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAYhJ8Gb1jwQ2wyMHTxlq4dLQI_gQhDBDhW3r5Khm698U463X0_l_ViYor59m021N2XFRhIyjnlfHUmptcqB6c9IqAYUPNZcAFSKTYEbUD5s1UnMYnr5Lk3woOc-YkppezMYYj63cY_ElKo8ezQguKTOF_NpJQnkqGLvdTxXmLuAX16lSfW0ZZtSDr0_mhEfBFYCCzHcYwV9461vfO3U2pyBJa9EVjuu7shdWijQ3oE7AEr7XXfHo2GvQW6lYwvakt-RttjydSaTFs32dG_04zAMlTtHkdMKbZpJpLdNhPTZ1H_j9BTw28AAAD__4C1Z-0) whether President-elect Donald Trump's so-called "hush money" conviction will be overturned in light of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on presidential immunity. The U.S. Supreme Court ruled in July that former [presidents can't be prosecuted](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGutCAUhJ8GOwwcULGg-Bu7_dtbGsXjejYgBnDNvv2N29-pJzNfvp_H-JjI0_78PwW0ZY0jCNBcSi7luEzkP6OuFrsiAqoKreyUVKCNEdVmO7cI3YheK6VmmDvh-sbNTdv0jcFVmorsvSa_UVqZul_QgZGt6uepNWZlWuBCJab6FWkvG67-E9-YahdD5e1WypGZ-sdgYDBc11VnF8uZZx-fd4XBcB8wGETHYHiduZDDzNPpkZd0hoNvU-Y5BuQUwrlT-fA1xcCPFDO6s1DcGQxVsstxkfeU245p8QwT-S9D-cvJ28JvAAAA__-arWPD) for actions related to their core presidential powers. Merchan will also decide whether to sentence Trump on Nov. 26 as currently scheduled, though experts suggest a sentence before his inauguration is unlikely. Trump's conviction in this case is one of [four legal battles](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvwyAQhX8N3rB8QA0eGLp4S9eOFoFzctUZLJsG5d9Xyd63vOFJT5--78tyCcSUb19hQ1_XsqhBGQkgAZYUiJ-L6ZJfERXqDj1YDVoZ54bu7t1qtbZ2mtzoRgMpDOAGrT4SXlcNCTvyrzd4Rxvt-ilhVA5GPV3D6NwqzICJajn6n0K53nHlZ3ng0ceydezvte6n0J9CzULNrbU-X2PGdr52oea9MFWKp1BzKjlwkvX43Xah5ndLZIyVSpaNsowHbZQDyxhOPCXjLbA8Yg5g3QS2O3zaGzHTOVphhtsWiN8c9T8vD6_-AgAA__-o5WWn) to which the President-elect has pleaded not guilty. The Justice Department is currently reviewing how to wind down the other federal cases.
## Shooting at Tuskegee University Homecoming
A [shooting at Alabama’s Tuskegee](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuhDAQRL_GdCB77QNTuIgU0V2aKEqJ1niBTQw-ge9O_H3E9Zl2NKOn933tr8iR1-kDF3J5TD1IMKVSpVJ9QI5Hb4rgRiIgXZBTjVYajLWymB1qoMsI3vqWGolNO4ag61qCNdpD6wt255t6RRttqzbQAFbVuvVYWzsKIylwTlv1k3jNM43xSA_aqiEtRXRzzrdd6DcBnYAO_bDSc6-mdNYCuq9PAd0-p5R5ncp8339pIirvKz9o2zkfJUb0uGA5p4WGtPA6nYuctkPojoPQ70pdGllbuBSbC7cnx8h73QgjpwU5vjjyf14eDv4CAAD__7-FZKk) University early Sunday morning resulted in one death and 16 injuries as the school was concluding its 100th Homecoming Week. The deceased victim was identified as an [18-year-old male](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOszAQhJ8GOiJ7vWBTuPibdPnbK9GC17B3BkfgJMrbn0h_U81opNHo-7oNN5Ik2_yfVvYl5gEUYKN1o_UQSNJ7wDr4yAxsavbaGm0AnVP14sGxmpQOqEZrIwH2NrhILnYGLaOpxZ9r-iODxl36wBM43Zl-pM65WKHiICXvl-8sW1k4pnd-8n6Z8lonv5RyPyrzr4JrBVe6b_w6zuoMe5EpcQXX8jh-eGZuHps8eT-kvBtKNNJKzbHkXGSbmyWvPOX1tOMYNUJUrSaOGlywvWottgapZWNVvftwf0lKcnS2QjWvJOlzqPwF6OnhNwAA__9BBGcs). His name has yet to be released, but authorities confirmed that he was not a student at the university. Twelve of the injuries were gunshot wounds, and at least two of the injured were students. name: Hillsdale College via 1600 Daily
subject: Help us understand CRT’s influence
body:
I’m reaching out because you’re an informed patriot who cares about this country.

This sponsored message from 1600 Daily is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z31ht126of2o9qc9eplhur8n8nl0t9kkpfeft5m8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmcpb6c1h2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zps9srln7mv1n3ffauesalpbu3bjhbsjh52c0s7g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmcpb6c1h2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z3ovb0hnq2j7u0lqp6sf2obcam2sq16gej07e95o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmcpb6c1h2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z4a9njiqu4faojkq9nk4aj18108sd533rokvrkgo_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmcpb6c1h2bs)
---
| |
---

1600 Daily

name: OKGOP
subject: Veterans We Can Never Thank You Enough!
body:
| [ ](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=urG6o0YzHACIJY0G92IOaIOiUFGhm1XQHaiZ59fvCoY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_veterans_we_can_never_thank&n=1)
---
Peter --
This Veterans Day, we pause to honor and thank the brave men and women who have served our nation in uniform. From the beaches of Normandy to the deserts of the Middle East, and from humanitarian missions to the front lines of combat, American veterans have dedicated themselves to the defense of our nation and the principles it upholds. Veterans Day is an opportunity to express our deepest gratitude for their sacrifice, commitment, and resilience. Veterans Day originated as Armistice Day on November 11, 1919, marking the first anniversary of the end of World War I. This day, chosen for the symbolic armistice signed in 1918, was intended to honor the courage and sacrifice of those who had served in "the war to end all wars." In 1954, after World War II and the Korean War, Congress officially changed "Armistice Day" to "Veterans Day" to honor all American veterans, regardless of the era in which they served. Since then, November 11 has been a day to recognize and thank veterans across all branches and all generations, from World War I to present-day conflicts. The contributions of our veterans go far beyond their time in uniform. After returning home, many veterans continue to serve their communities in countless ways—through public service, leadership, volunteer work, and as steadfast friends and family members. Their dedication to service is a testament to the values they hold dear, values that define our country and inspire us all. Veterans have left an indelible mark on our nation’s history and culture. They are woven into the fabric of our communities, representing the strength, resilience, and unity that keep our nation strong. They have faced unimaginable challenges and shown extraordinary courage. Their experiences and sacrifices have helped shape our nation’s freedom, safety, and democracy. Each veteran’s story is a part of America’s collective story—a story of bravery, perseverance, and love of country. As we honor our veterans this year, we must also recognize the responsibility we bear to support them. It is our duty to ensure they have access to the resources and care they deserve, from quality healthcare and mental health services to educational opportunities and job assistance. Too often, veterans face challenges transitioning back to civilian life, and it is our responsibility as a nation to support them as they supported us. Veterans Day is also a time to remember those who made the ultimate sacrifice in the line of duty. We remember them and honor their families, whose own sacrifices are no less significant. The freedom we enjoy today is possible because of their bravery and selflessness. Today, I encourage all of us to take a moment to reflect on the sacrifices made by veterans and their families and to express our gratitude. If you know a veteran, consider reaching out with a message of thanks. Small gestures of appreciation go a long way in letting veterans know that their service is not forgotten. To all veterans: thank you for your service, your sacrifices, and your enduring commitment to our nation. We honor you today and every day. If you would like another way to show your support, go to this link and see about helping disabled veterans all across the country: [https://www.dav.org/ ](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=ljf7ubM8wMfXkViqyWPmpG5ioPTRqMRCgtWyhdiD8MI&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_veterans_we_can_never_thank&n=2) From your friends at the OKGOP,
God Bless

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/unsubscribe?e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_veterans_we_can_never_thank&n=3).
You can also keep up with OKGOP on [Twitter](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=JcrYNQ7dhdufXPgOgJxNGHSsRONv6mnq7ly6aBOUvGVprSj7jfHNOpP70kR_1QLA&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_veterans_we_can_never_thank&n=4) or [Facebook](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=UQNagEd-DqbIXa37T8ODid9mG9NGbq3z9te4_riKc6EaGmrOrRzKc2wAaVTFbzRY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_veterans_we_can_never_thank&n=5).

name: Hillsdale College via Trump News HQ
subject: Take our Critical Race Theory Survey
body:
I’m reaching out because you’re an informed patriot who cares about this country.

This sponsored message from Trump Train News is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zpj0gnnqrvl5mrngnl0s3i34mc2laau6m84gdhj8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6c1h2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zuhsam1eivo1osuo0sfq0n23m762enqvl1vftjro_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6c1h2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zqu69fj6p49vct825qme63gs6vl4i3ndounogb9o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6c1h2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zhfnfrhqg9i9o1k5t4ctb6jdr91o5q9liuse2dro_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6c1h2bs)
---
| |
---

---

name: Jim Jordan
subject: I’m handing the reins of our Party over to YOU
body:
It’s time for Republicans to come together and let’s get to work to make America great again.

It’s time for Republicans to come together and let’s get to work to make America great again.
| [](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2ziilrkh5t7ob3upf5vaggtg5ofe67mq3fov5ulno_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6cjh2bs)
---
Friend,
---
During the 1984 election, Ronald Reagan proclaimed, “It’s morning in America.”
---
That’s how I feel today.
---
President Donald J. Trump once again defied the media and the Washington ruling class by ushering in a historic victory.
---
This election was a clear mandate from the American people to charter a new path forward for our nation.
---
_The people spoke – and now it’s time for your elected officials to deliver!_
---
That’s why I’m handing the reins of our Party over to voters like _YOU_ to help craft our new Republican agenda to truly make America great again.
---
[**Please take the People’s Agenda Survey as your Republican elected officials come together to put America on track for a bright new future.**](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zi379r60mkmpj4sdv2h7o2o4ue91en4uvaq46f3g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6cjh2bs)
---

[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zbr4gol9huig2ig7n5iu72054t20uuda8fnuhh38_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6cjh2bs)
---

If there’s one takeaway from the election, it’s that the American people are tired of being lectured to by an out-of-touch political elite that don’t have our interests in mind.
---
But the Silent Majority is silent _no more!_
---
With President Trump returning to the White House, a new Republican Senate majority, and Republicans on track to keep the House, your voice, your concerns, and your priorities will be heard loud and clear in our nation’s capital.
---
And that starts right here, right now.
---
That’s why I’m asking you to take my FIRST survey so that your new united Republican government can be certain that we’re fighting for the issues that voters like you care about the most.
---
[**Please take the People’s Agenda Survey as your Republican elected officials come together to put America on track for a bright new future.**](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2ztb7elevj5gmcvadvhtlknndj3a4e6pk2uhfpo7g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6cjh2bs)
---
Thank you – and let’s get to work and save our country.
---
[](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2za37ql2fgqotansvfsu1277jvp81leuhf6r1mb6o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm2ob6cjh2bs)
---
Jim Jordan
---

---
Paid for by Team Jordan a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed between Jim Jordan for Congress, Buckeye Liberty Fund, and American Liberty Foundation PAC.

PO Box 909, Marysville, OH 43040
---
---

American Defense News

name: Hillsdale College via Your America News
subject: Help us understand CRT’s influence
body:
I’m reaching out because you’re an informed patriot who cares about this country.

This sponsored message from Your America News is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zek4qmirp59ucvvqebgf2uuj6851bpa9alcomllg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zq1pbibhnofa0h7cqh71o9jkcg2tlssiarco2kbo_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z1o1sjuicj9mep40voru7pc5e75t8268381d4cn8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zlpk41pom5hikpu6hee7l07va3dt2j8ei47sle0g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph2bs)
---
| |
---

Your America
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z9j0gfnbuvav61kb5nclt3cp8mtgv3f5brg7ga2g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pq9ttle9q3bd9ieir38ct3mkrnctt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8pj5cdg69p324no)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z19bkruetfofiqeeli23vugpljavbrp63go79p8g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgmapj1cph2bs)_

name: Dave McCormick
subject: Here’s what I shared w/ Dana Perino & Bill Hemmer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick
body:
In case you missed it

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zt02s97ckqt9rts41oe1afi6hc6bbg5ou82l5s3o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)

Friend,

In case you missed it, here's the bottom line up front.

I joined Dana Perino and Bill Hemmer on _America 's Newsroom_ to discuss my big win in the Pennsylvania senate race.

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zepadmqilhjvsj7io4qb6505eji7uki1oltdr1uo_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)

**[WATCH DAVE ON _AMERICA 'S NEWSROOM_](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn00npjnc2jfkal6up7b1abhfbpiunjk8sqvtrb8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)**

Here's what I shared with Dana and Bill.

✔ **Pennsylvanians voted for CHANGE.** They don't want incremental change. They want significant change, and that's what I intend to do when I get to Washington.

✔ **Authenticity.** People want leaders who do what they say they will do. And if they make a mistake, they own it. I've said it many times over the course of the campaign; I live by the West Point cadet motto: _Duty, Honor, Country. _

✔ **The election is over, and I 'm leading by over 32,000 votes.** But that hasn't stopped my opponent Bob Casey and the national Democrat machine from organizing and fundraising to try and force a recount. Pennsylvania law dictates an automatic recount if the margin is less than .5%. I'm currently leading by EXACTLY .5%, and we expect my opponent to try and force a recount.

But we're following the lead of President Trump. We are going to **FIGHT! FIGHT! FIGHT! **

**[While votes continue to be counted, I'm asking for you to chip in today to help us keep up our fight.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z060vo3nt3k3kd3491f86n3r86j0cb0cfttikpf8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)**

_If you are not in a position to give right now, please don 't. I mean it. _

But if you are able to give, [I'm humbly asking you to chip in any amount to help us fund whatever the Democrats will throw at us in the coming days.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1j4h6gfalnopvsng1toge2ucf36b6cdob96emag_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)

Thank you for all of your support. We couldn't have done this without you! I know how to restore strong leadership in our country because I've done it before. And I'll do it as your next Senator.

Thanks again,
_Dave McCormick_
[DONATE](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeciso13k5q565307t78qapf5rs38oi93qb9vpg8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4cpgm6oj4c1h2bs)

But with Bob Casey and liberal lawyers plotting a recount, Dave McCormick needs your support. Thanks, Ted Cruz Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick.
