"I was surprised to find that the densely packed packages were easy to prepare and were tasty as well. I definitely recommend having survival food on hand for the times when 'life' happens while we are planning other things." **Billy H.**
"Received my food kit, and I was able to make the potato soup on my cookstove. Just needed boiling water. Gave it a good stir and let it simmer. When I opened up the pot, it smelled so good. You can see the chunks of potatoes and carrots in each bite you take. It's a good soup." **Kevin S.**
"The 72 hour kit is a perfect starter kit for any scenario. Put one in your car, work bag, in your house or RV. If you want to start preparing safe food, this is where to start." **Carol B.**
"I have been iced in this weekend. I thought I would try my potato soup. To my utter delight, IT WAS DELICIOUS. Thank you for such a wonderful product and the peace of mind it brings." **Ken K.**
"I am not a full-blown survivalist. I am not an idiot, either. I have been through enough in my life and have seen friends who have been through an emergency situation. Save up if you have to, but get at least a month's supply. It tastes good. Although if it's a dire of an emergency, you will be happy to eat anything. IF you have something to eat for your family. We made some of this product, and had family and friends over for them to taste. They all agreed they didn't think anything would be this good, and they will be ordering. Just do something. You can't miss out on this deal." **Justin A.**
"My wife and I tried the food and we were both surprised about how good it tasted and how satisfied it made us feel afterwards. It feels good knowing that I can provide for my family if a crisis arose and I intend to get more in the future. Also the shipping and customer service has been top notch. This is probably the cheapest survival food I have found, and the company is great." **Gary M.**
"I actually had lost my job and was homeless for a while. I dug into my food supply, and I cannot fully describe how delicious and easy to prepare everything is. I felt like I was eating like a king. I am going to stock up again as soon as I settle into my new job. Everyone should participate with this company. You will not be disappointed." **John H.**
"We're in Florida and have made many preparations for the aftermath of a possible hurricane. While we are thankful that Florida has not been hit in several years, it gives us great peace of mind to know we have our 4Patriots kit stashed away, knowing it's not a case of "if" but "when"." body:
# [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96941919/511720149/18334046?refcode=UTS_FR_90DO_241111_1_1_Thankful&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5NDc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjdkNzUzMTdiLTZmYTAtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Hk836VhPz992VVFy4BPB6DclABy1q4rlaGkQmlIsyCs=)
Team,
President Biden and Vice President Harris have made historic progress for our nation. They’ve fought tirelessly to create millions of jobs, improve healthcare access, protect our environment, and rebuild our infrastructure. We owe them our thanks for their dedication to moving our country forward.
Now, let’s show them how grateful we are. Add your name to our thank-you message and let President Biden and Vice President Harris know that you stand with them! ↙️
Dear President Biden and Vice President Harris,
We want to take a moment to express our gratitude for your steadfast leadership and dedication over the past few years. Your tireless work has led to remarkable achievements that uplift Americans across the nation.
From revitalizing our infrastructure to expanding healthcare access and championing environmental protections, your administration has made an undeniable difference. You have shown what a government truly committed to progress and equality can accomplish, and we are deeply appreciative of your efforts.
Thank you for your service and for inspiring us all to believe in a brighter, fairer future.
With deepest gratitude,
x[Your Name]

name: Dan Carr
subject: Health System In America Needs Reform
body:
Help is on the WAY!!

| |
---|---|---
|
# Health System In America Needs Reform
### Help is on the WAY!!
| | | [Dan Carr](https://substack.com/@dancarr2014)
---
| Nov 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@dancarr2014)
---
In this video, we discuss the implications of recent political shifts in the U.S., examining the election results and new leadership changes in our healthcare system.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/947006bf-b578-4227-8932-2aade3fa043b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
As we explore the potential impact of these developments, we'll analyze what this means for everyday Americans, healthcare policies, and the broader future of health in our nation. Stay informed and join the conversation! Peter, It's Dave McCormick. The AP called our race and we won! But Democrats refuse to accept the results and I need your help. Please rush in a donation to help us keep liberal lawyers like Marc Elias out of Pennsylvania! The election is over. But that hasn't stopped my opponent Bob Casey and the national Democrat machine from organizing and fundraising to try and force a recount. Pennsylvania law dictates an automatic recount if the margin is less than .5%. I'm currently leading by EXACTLY .5%, and we expect my opponent to try and force a recount. If you are not in a position to give right now, please don 't. I mean it. But Bob Casey and the national Democrats won't accept it. And we're fighting back. We have attorneys who have been deployed to fight back. We can't be caught flat-footed and allow liberals to steal this election. Thank you, Dave McCormick Paid for by Friends of Dave McCormick. _**A message from our sponsor...**_ | | | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=2a06db40288143ca98d685e7db34984c&_e=ZTWfnqDnV4BuiOMoYHW8b9yrYC3FWBCzsLQ8Y9VpCdcMlcQeQbl1OZ6_tVbYDSE_9kSfLJMcy9sozEcaCnMdiZozRdDQJPRB6UefQZbLsIiu3VmCiglDw3_4NCLMjj3bA01zWGU5KxkogH03H-sRxjUbYPDhOYf7QS-rS4aeEJ-T4XLDiWWghktCIYDNbrmeFPvpKeKLMFDGccOSnIbtaxaimuTttWnJeHLwzA1u0C8eX1bNBHpxUWZgzoWq5nwJZ520AAXvGx9YaDuRqsxTvuaSi5mam-FTW0dGCnghpNHvT_3PvYVeRUOwNSF2DuJvPuWeqZELJcMj7I6FS6Isn9y9YXQgUoJp6RdiLxNvAUoygsK6hcWNRRhXbyoSBFg1_z6OEgr6Zc90_goQPl9kNXuBYlr50yZAT4pdZxKFqWWCqwHEusfibDKQyRukM7FJ)

| | Dear Friend, C.S. Lewis was a master at addressing life's most important questions through vivid language that is delightful and captivating to read.

His signature ability to communicate the truth about Christianity has inspired countless skeptics to become believers and even more believers to renew and deepen their faith--making him the greatest Christian apologist of the twentieth century.

To help you learn more about Lewis, we produced "[**C.S. Lewis on Christianity,**](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=2a06db40288143ca98d685e7db34984c&_e=ZTWfnqDnV4BuiOMoYHW8b9yrYC3FWBCzsLQ8Y9VpCdcMlcQeQbl1OZ6_tVbYDSE_9kSfLJMcy9sozEcaCnMdiZozRdDQJPRB6UefQZbLsIiu3VmCiglDw3_4NCLMjj3bA01zWGU5KxkogH03H-sRxjUbYPDhOYf7QS-rS4aeEJ-T4XLDiWWghktCIYDNbrmeFPvpKeKLMFDGccOSnIbtaxaimuTttWnJeHLwzA1u0C8eX1bNBHpxUWZgzoWq5nwJZ520AAXvGx9YaDuRqsxTvoh-QaPykBZWzw1jVlcAN8rTOHcZLHZP7tYw3A8UmedN2nJeCKKnZiMBzrmkfpASkiUl9W-3-m5Ztur-VLzOITeODoNutRVNxq3C5eccwDbuUD4SGNFoDgqOyu0AICZUBvhMIeNO8h2fEcM5uT5ElX5t2bAE7jwL12lIuDenWoTq)" which you can now own on DVD.

| [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=2a06db40288143ca98d685e7db34984c&_e=ZTWfnqDnV4BuiOMoYHW8b9yrYC3FWBCzsLQ8Y9VpCdcMlcQeQbl1OZ6_tVbYDSE_9kSfLJMcy9sozEcaCnMdiZozRdDQJPRB6UefQZbLsIiu3VmCiglDw3_4NCLMjj3bA01zWGU5KxkogH03H-sRxjUbYPDhOYf7QS-rS4aeEJ-T4XLDiWWghktCIYDNbrmeFPvpKeKLMFDGccOSnIbtaxaimuTttWnJeHLwzA1u0C8eX1bNBHpxUWZgzoWq5nwJZ520AAXvGx9YaDuRqsxTvtq-fVlDsgtFRLnyuNOLOTxFlrdxMZaQDe3aF-S6AB1CkjQ4XkUsOZqHkbbr4jRYW_Z949XUzRu_M8ydtyOhwIR6KI_eW0fpXl-4xp-WA8cEuNMyUHjh_UKTjYqtWrrkhrQc7cxyzJ7MdwA49uAmXC7f_Rrrnx8YsRCth6Lh5oES)

| | In this course you will learn Lewis's profound insights on the central topics of Christianity, including:
* good and evil,
* conversion and faith,
* prayer and the Bible,
* suffering and death,
* heaven and hell, and much more.

The instructor, Dr. Michael Ward, is a Distinguished Visiting Professor at Hillsdale College and a member of the Faculty of Theology and Religion at the University of Oxford. He is the author of _Planet Narnia: The Seven Heavens in the Imagination of C.S. Lewis_ and _After Humanity: A Guide to C.S. Lewis 's The Abolition of Man_, and the co-editor of _The Cambridge Companion to C.S. Lewis_. He even spent three years living in Lewis's Oxford home as the resident Warden of The Kilns!

This course is especially moving because it examines C.S. Lewis's beautiful writings about faith and traces Lewis's own journey from skeptic to believer. The combination provides an opportunity for us to grow in our knowledge and understanding of Christianity.

That's why I hope you'll join us in examining the eternal questions of Christianity with C.S. Lewis with [this beautiful DVD box set](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=2a06db40288143ca98d685e7db34984c&_e=ZTWfnqDnV4BuiOMoYHW8b9yrYC3FWBCzsLQ8Y9VpCdcMlcQeQbl1OZ6_tVbYDSE_9kSfLJMcy9sozEcaCnMdiZozRdDQJPRB6UefQZbLsIiu3VmCiglDw3_4NCLMjj3bA01zWGU5KxkogH03H-sRxjUbYPDhOYf7QS-rS4aeEJ-T4XLDiWWghktCIYDNbrmeFPvpKeKLMFDGccOSnIbtaxaimuTttWnJeHLwzA1u0C8eX1bNBHpxUWZgzoWq5nwJZ520AAXvGx9YaDuRqsxTvqn6bpnNyhsTWfUXMOGnv64Wl23BN34FnOf1hq3gA6Mn1M4B30xm4pZbWJAcKsOf6tAk9vBGjFxAQlWO_d14FtZVGOOuOIl6RCjjkzUxQhMLoBmV0sbUzrVuTP45l3ujyZ-YodL6LEB0i_rCOU-uHEONhSerZSUMN4zgdZ-nhJpT).

Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College [Unsubscribe](http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=2a06db40288143ca98d685e7db34984c&_t=KL61XKT0IfSUD6oZWd4n7qKMdTzJIdR1L2-aGicckvZ4OOfrr0UhpuT4VlmRXuDaQMWzS9JW1LoN9KuSKnKzyIBgxUXa3xVdSce80vwvthv0RCVAJxNGxf_-NIIsLrGq)

subject: making sure every vote is counted
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
| |
---
Peter, this is big: Senator Bob Casey is still in the fight of his career to make sure _**ANOTHER**_ MAGA extremist doesn’t head to the Senate next year. So we need all hands on deck **RIGHT NOW** to make sure every single vote is counted. **[We urgently need your help to make sure every ballot in this race is counted correctly. Don’t wish you did more – chip in $10 or any amount right away.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96942753/511723154/-175117156?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_PADM_20241111_FR_Recount_0_3MO_PADems_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ3MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmQxNGU2Ny03MmEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HFhD5DszD-q_YPG0yjTnLsBu5UWwjJL_kWxBagX5dn4=)** | [RUSH A DONATION NOW >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96942754/511723155/510837584?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_PADM_20241111_FR_Recount_0_3MO_PADems_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ3MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmQxNGU2Ny03MmEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HFhD5DszD-q_YPG0yjTnLsBu5UWwjJL_kWxBagX5dn4=)
---
It has never been more important for us to hold onto this seat. We need a leader like Bob Casey in the Senate to serve as an advocate for our rights and freedoms.

name: Shawnna Bolick
subject: We won AND the Republicans even added seats to our legislative majority
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTE BOLICK
body:
| | | [ ](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=93729a7e76&e=a7ab36849c)
---
| | Good afternoon Freedom Fighters,

This year has been quite the incredible journey for our household. I am so thankful for all the support my husband and I received for our respective elections. As you know, these last several months have been grueling. As always, I remained tenacious every day and it paid off. I humbly asked for your vote, and you all came through! I am currently leading my opponent by 3,766 votes. Therefore, I am the projected winner of the State Senate seat for District 2.

I cannot thank our family, friends, and supporters enough for helping to push us over the finish line. I am fortunate to have had helping hands to complete our post card writing campaigns in both the primary and the general elections. We also had to deliver campaign literature to voters in all the precincts targeting registered voters across all political parties. We had volunteers from all over the state making phone calls and sending positive text messages to our district’s voters. Thank you to everyone who reached out to help us, maximizing our efforts to reaching our goal of winning this seat.

Once again, out-of-state big money Leftists tried to buy our state legislature. Thankfully, common sense prevailed. The Republican legislative majority gained seats. The county election offices are still counting votes, but this is terrific news since the Republicans were projected to lose seats, especially in swing districts like the one I represent.

As you can imagine, running a state legislative political campaign in a swing district is not easy, but throwing a judicial retention into the mix wasn’t what I call fun. Sadly, my husband will reach his final year to serve before reaching mandatory retirement on the Arizona Supreme Court in three years.

It has truly been an honor to serve in the State Senate since I was unanimously appointed in July 2023. I have kept two parking receipts from my meetings with the Maricopa County Board of Supervisors attached to my car’s visor reminding me each day I get into my car that I needed to earn my entire district’s support in 2024, not just a handful of Republican precinct committeemen and five Maricopa County Board of Supervisors.

I very much look forward to this next chapter continuing to represent District 2 for two full years. Since I won a two-year term, I will likely give myself a month away from campaigning, but after the start of the new year we must start collecting signatures to qualify for the 2026 primary ballot and of course, raising money to invest in direct mail, digital ads, etc. to run a successful campaign.

Thank you so much for your continued support.

Best,
Shawnna

P.S. Tomorrow, I will participate in electing Senate leadership for 2025-20226.

---
| |
---
| | | | | | | [](https://bolickforarizona.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=3214fdbd528e32d06cf84fc86&id=6cc01dc239&e=a7ab36849c)

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: I’m honored and humbled to continue serving as Nevada’s United States Senator
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
Thank you
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96942978/511723986/-229028829?refcode=jtk5421-ot-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5OTgwZGFjZC03NGEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hjllxktvWurgaJALE7dWOMyR6_X0QmK-_nDTtfWir0Q=&emci=67dab891-ad9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=9980dacd-74a0-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3800590)
Hello friend,
I’m honored and humbled by the trust Nevada voters have put in me to continue serving as their United States Senator.
Thank you to everyone who donated – giving whatever you could to make sure we had the resources to get our message out and win. Thank you to the dedicated supporters, friends, and volunteers who have spent countless hours organizing, mobilizing, getting out the vote, and curing ballots.
We still have a lot of big challenges ahead of us, but I know we can keep making progress for the American people together.
I’ll keep fighting to level the playing field for all workers.
I’ll keep fighting against the Big Oil CEOs and big grocery store chains who are raking in record profits while raising prices at the gas pump and at the checkout line.
I’ll keep fighting to build more housing that regular people can afford.
I’ll keep fighting to restore _Roe v. Wade_ – and I will NEVER back down in the fight against extreme Republican efforts to further roll back our rights.
I hope you will join me.
Thank you for this opportunity – I won’t let you down.
All my best,

Jacky Rosen

name: Larry Kudlow
subject: The New York Sun’s Urgent Mission
body:
As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.
It's a voice America needs

| | | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=f93fa70120a5492db6cebd2e2ea2bff3&_e=Q8HEOhc4iBcyJxcONxP17kXk_D399VRCIGuuRXjqjKurxiEZhEpYLvKXfgKEkmzKzck1txSWPy02aJuzigpRp9NPoUId7BawiyLwprfxRVFZ0qmDNuq-LPsuWeoHRmgsx9DhWcfs0XCfAUaXzLwnvLSKoQHThDvkpoVeOKEl6BdV7p_BZAtIMrLTP_BjpOejx_zZTOW2KuRomKGfQl3M8Q2a7egfGHLoW8I_Rm0HuIOXLOh4ZfAH4WctX79TItk9gv25m7zMljqfScAFm4bVQegIDAQ0jVtvDUT_TIqydqrtpS1v_BuZ4xoberItOnfQ2Qf-0zqsDiWvq4LjnL3VTM_Bf8xwU3KjzpgF_PUh-B2nMFf3TpJpgNVM8F_OFsy1120DtHTccl57dSJmqm236Q%3D%3D)

---
# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Peter,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.

clean_body: As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be. It's a voice America needs | | | | FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW | | Peter,I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns. I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism. But there's also far more to what the Sun is building. As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be. I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust. The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored. The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way. The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun. A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly. I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths. They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts. | | | | Please join me in the fight to restore America's journalism. This is one mission that our country desperately needs. | | | Larry Kudlow Columnist for The New York Sun | | | | | Larry Kudlow Columnist for The New York Sun | | |

name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Take back the libraries
body:
Well... Times have certainly changed.

| | | Well... Times have certainly changed.
---
| |
---
| | [](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilIGlnyHnxIsL2UJaWYkB6F5JkkIv4TZHlL376k2zxArz9mhvl938-jO_o4xvn63U9U55RdWl1AF5L_JH6P6c7HGJJPv50SSnMpuZQOCqy1VQimoFpakGAsCFHcaoWDBhQoSErj5QVLuIhyqLASAUpDRayfd6SUUkldgd29Vd54oUJpATVqZFr4kVJed_lGiR5bGOPg50x-2g3LVIz1LefHymDPVMdUt9KwJdr5mXDJzwmmuq_vw7LcV6Y6tGjJkOWVxYFrLAN_u4iSozGgy4uUMggG3ZYnty5bGohBc2jeed-emTJPPBHGbWLQtMd_ZPDTw8frzKD58hGVOzTv7tDvz637-NZ_gGuPrm_P7seen_rTa2-ZM82ZQXPqT_wVRZFqfPyK4xhXY5kW18nH8a9t_o9OPmv1JwAA__-j54_N)
---
Back when you were growing up, you may remember lazy Saturdays at the library discovering classic stories like _Treasure Island_ and _Charlotte's Web_?Well... Times have certainly changed. Nowadays the president of the American Library Association (ALA) is a self-described "Marxist lesbian." Radical librarians are calling _Huckleberry Finn_ and _To Kill a Mockingbird_ "racist" and are promoting books that push anti-family, activist messages to young children.
---
| | Instead of discovering the classics like Treasure Island and Charlotte's Web, they want kids to go to "Drag Queen Story Hours" that feature books like _Woke Baby_ , _A is for Activist_ , and _The Hips on the Drag Queen Go Swish, Swish, Swish_ \-- teaching children to "raise their fists" and embrace radical ideologies.
---
| [](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilIGlnyHnxIsL2UJaWYkB6F5JkkIv4TZHlL376k2zxArz9mhvl938-jO_o4xvn63U9U55RdWl1AF5L_JH6P6c7HGJJPv50SSnMpuZQOCqy1VQimoFpakGAsCFHcaoWDBhQoSErj5QVLuIhyqLASAUpDRayfd6SUUkldgd29Vd54oUJpATVqZFr4kVJed_lGiR5bGOPg50x-2g3LVIz1LefHymDPVMdUt9KwJdr5mXDJzwmmuq_vw7LcV6Y6tGjJkOWVxYFrLAN_u4iSozGgy4uUMggG3ZYnty5bGohBc2jeed-emTJPPBHGbWLQtMd_ZPDTw8frzKD58hGVOzTv7tDvz637-NZ_gGuPrm_P7seen_rTa2-ZM82ZQXPqT_wVRZFqfPyK4xhXY5kW18nH8a9t_o9OPmv1JwAA__-j54_N)
---
| | This is happening in libraries across the country, not just in liberal cities. **Our taxpayer-funded libraries are grooming a new generation to reject Godly and conservative values.** That's why we need your help to [**Take Back Our Libraries**.](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilIGlnyHnxIsL2UJaWYkB6F5JkkIv4TZHlL376k2zxArz9mhvl938-jO_o4xvn63U9U55RdWl1AF5L_JH6P6c7HGJJPv50SSnMpuZQOCqy1VQimoFpakGAsCFHcaoWDBhQoSErj5QVLuIhyqLASAUpDRayfd6SUUkldgd29Vd54oUJpATVqZFr4kVJed_lGiR5bGOPg50x-2g3LVIz1LefHymDPVMdUt9KwJdr5mXDJzwmmuq_vw7LcV6Y6tGjJkOWVxYFrLAN_u4iSozGgy4uUMggG3ZYnty5bGohBc2jeed-emTJPPBHGbWLQtMd_ZPDTw8frzKD58hGVOzTv7tDvz637-NZ_gGuPrm_P7seen_rTa2-ZM82ZQXPqT_wVRZFqfPyK4xhXY5kW18nH8a9t_o9OPmv1JwAA__-j54_N) My name is Kirk Cameron. With Brave Books, I created _As You Grow_ , teaching children to grow in wisdom through faith. Together, we've launched wholesome Story Hours, but radical librarians and the ALA have tried to shut us out, scheming to keep us from hosting events.Despite this, thousands of families have joined us at our Story Hours, pledging allegiance, singing "God Bless America," and sharing Godly values through wholesome stories.Now, Brave Books has started the "See You at the Library" movement to empower parents, grandparents, and patriots like you to host Story Hours at your local libraries.Our mission is simple: **reclaim childhood innocence and preserve family values.** To reach our goal of 100,000 children next year, we need your help to send "Story Hour Kits" to every committed patriot. Each kit includes books, flags, stickers, Bible verse cards, and everything needed to bring traditional values to public libraries. **[**Will you sponsor a Story Hour Kit with a gift of $35, $50, $75, or $100?**](https://email.alerts.therepublicanteam.com/c/eJyUkc-K2zAQxp9GvilIGlnyHnxIsL2UJaWYkB6F5JkkIv4TZHlL376k2zxArz9mhvl938-jO_o4xvn63U9U55RdWl1AF5L_JH6P6c7HGJJPv50SSnMpuZQOCqy1VQimoFpakGAsCFHcaoWDBhQoSErj5QVLuIhyqLASAUpDRayfd6SUUkldgd29Vd54oUJpATVqZFr4kVJed_lGiR5bGOPg50x-2g3LVIz1LefHymDPVMdUt9KwJdr5mXDJzwmmuq_vw7LcV6Y6tGjJkOWVxYFrLAN_u4iSozGgy4uUMggG3ZYnty5bGohBc2jeed-emTJPPBHGbWLQtMd_ZPDTw8frzKD58hGVOzTv7tDvz637-NZ_gGuPrm_P7seen_rTa2-ZM82ZQXPqT_wVRZFqfPyK4xhXY5kW18nH8a9t_o9OPmv1JwAA__-j54_N)** A gift of **$175** sponsors one entire kit, reaching 50 children with wholesome, patriotic messages. The kit includes everything they need to bring your conservative values back into our public libraries: instructions for booking their library space and running their Story Hour, small American flags for the little ones, fun stickers, wholesome books, Bible memory verse cards, and other specialty items.Thank you for helping us protect our libraries and restore Godly values.God bless America,
---
| | Kirk CameronActor, Author, and Father

name: Urgent Request
subject: URGENT: Volunteers needed in Pennsylvania
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hi there,

Volunteers are urgently needed in Pennsylvania. With tens of thousands of votes still to be counted, Senator Bob Casey's race is still too close to call. **While votes are still being counted, you can help ensure that Pennsylvanians' voices are heard.**

Thousands of already-cast votes have been rejected by their County Board of Elections, but we have the chance to help voters correct their ballots. We're counting on you to help ensure Pennsylvanians' voices are heard by contacting these voters and explaining how these mistakes can be corrected – a process called "curing."

[**Will you sign up for a shift to help cure ballots in-person or virtually? Voters only have until TOMORROW, November 12th to correct issues with their ballots – so time is running out:**](https://go.dscc.org/go/20733?t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241111-UrgentRequest-PAVolunteerAsk-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241111-UrgentRequest-PAVolunteerAsk-Pros-sl1&akid=18912%2E9716520%2EmGWDFY)

| | [Volunteer Now](https://go.dscc.org/go/20733?t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241111-UrgentRequest-PAVolunteerAsk-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241111-UrgentRequest-PAVolunteerAsk-Pros-sl1&akid=18912%2E9716520%2EmGWDFY)
---

If you are located in or near Pennsylvania, the most effective way to volunteer is to knock on doors. If you are unable to canvass, there are virtual options to call voters from your own home. The Pennsylvania Democrats team will train you before your volunteer shift begins, and will be available to answer any questions you have.

Thank you so much for your support at this critical time,

Team DSCC
| |
* * *
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate, and our work relies on grassroots supporters like you. If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please **[click here](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-senate-democrats-2024-q4?refcode=DPE-footer&t=1&refcode2=18912_9716520_mGWDFY&akid=18912%2E9716520%2EmGWDFY)**.

**[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=18912%2E9716520%2EmGWDFY)** to receive fewer emails. **[Click here](https://go.dscc.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=18912%2E9716520%2EmGWDFY)** to unsubscribe.

Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc.org](https://www.dscc.org/)** | Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

Contributions or gifts to the DSCC are not tax deductible.

name: sydney@uso.org
subject: Did you hear that all gifts to the USO are being MATCHED for Veterans Day?
body:
I don't want you to miss this:
USO Supporter,
I wanted to make sure you saw Christopher Plamp's message sent earlier today.
If you haven't had the chance to make a gift yet this Veterans Day, it's not too late! Take advantage of our match offer from our partners at the NFL* and have the value of _every single dollar_ you give be worth TWICE AS MUCH to help provide our service members with essential resources and connections to home.
**[Can we count on you to support our military heroes now with a matched donation of $29 or more in honor of Veterans Day?](https://links.e.uso.)**
Thank you so much for your support.
Sydney Jones, USO
* * *
**From: **From the Desk of the COO
**Sent: **Monday, November 11, 2024
**Subject:** Double your support for service members this Veterans Day

USO Supporter,
Every year, we come together on Nov. 11 to honor our brave men and women who have served and continue to serve our great nation. It's a day to reflect on the sacrifices they have made and the freedoms they have protected. As someone who has worn the uniform, I can tell you firsthand just how meaningful that support is.
**This Veterans Day, your support can go even further. Thanks to our generous partners at the NFL, all gifts made to the USO will be matched, dollar for dollar, up to a total of $50,000, to help us reach our $100,000 email and text message fundraising goal -- but only for a limited time. **
**[Will you join me in making a gift of $29 or more now that will be MATCHED to support service members around the world making unimaginable sacrifices for us every day?](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/N-6VBp4YNjs~)**
As an Air Force veteran, I know what it's like when the USO is your only way to connect to a loved one back home, get essential supplies or enjoy a holiday meal. And I can tell you that it's no secret to service members where all that generous support comes from.
_Everything the USO does for our heroes in uniform is made possible by generous supporters like you._
**There 's no better time to show your gratitude. Rush your gift before midnight and double your support for the heroes who stand on the front lines for us every day. **
**[Will you make a donation to the USO right now that will be DOUBLED in value to support our service members as they selflessly serve our country?](https://links.e.uso.)**
[RUSH $29 (BECOMES $58)](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/jbd6CAg_)
---
[RUSH $58 (BECOMES $116)](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/A-)
---
[RUSH $116 (BECOMES $232)](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/Dd9Kh-qXy~)
---
[RUSH ANOTHER AMOUNT TO HAVE IT DOUBLED!](https://links.e.uso.)
---
Every moment we enjoy our freedoms is because of the dedication of our veterans and current service members. Your gift today will ensure that we can continue supporting those who defend our nation.
Christopher Plamp
Colonel, U.S. Air Force (Retired)
Chief Operating Officer, USO

body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96943565/511726189/-488448398?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241111_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTM3NjZmOS03OGEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k_bends9MaPpL-1uUuyxcirIPYAXRdqFGFn7RukAYzI=)
---
Peter: Last week was hard for all of us, but we simply don't have time to lick our wounds. We need to start preparing to stop the Trump 2025 agenda right away. I believe one of the first things Democrats need to do is form a Shadow Cabinet to oppose Donald Trump, and I wrote an **[op-ed in The Washington Post](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96943568/511726192/-1079958245?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241111_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTM3NjZmOS03OGEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k_bends9MaPpL-1uUuyxcirIPYAXRdqFGFn7RukAYzI=&emci=f569eaf0-77a0-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=413766f9-78a0-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=28008880)** to outline my plan. Please take a moment to read it so we can get to work together. — Wiley | **THE WASHINGTON POST**
**[What should Democrats do now? Form a shadow cabinet.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96943569/511726195/-1079958245?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241111_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTM3NjZmOS03OGEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k_bends9MaPpL-1uUuyxcirIPYAXRdqFGFn7RukAYzI=&emci=f569eaf0-77a0-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=413766f9-78a0-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=28008880)**
Opinion | Wiley Nickel Last week stung. As Democrats, we simply failed to convince the American people we have better ideas to solve their problems. Now, we need to dust ourselves off and get ready to fight. We can’t let Donald Trump’s radical agenda go unanswered or unopposed. Here’s an idea for how to organize our opposition: We need to borrow from our British friends and appoint a shadow cabinet to fight back against the worst excesses of a second Trump administration. The special relationship between the United States and Britain extends not just to our strength on the world stage but also to our shared belief in democracy. Across the Atlantic, the British have something we don’t: a team from the opposition that mirrors the government’s cabinet members. They watch the cabinet closely, publicly challenging, scrutinizing and offering new ideas. It’s another form of checks and balances — a quiet guardrail that keeps power accountable. With a shadow cabinet, there is no hiding. Each decision by the government faces a ready counterpoint — not just from the media and voters but also from political leaders poised to (theoretically) step in. Shadow cabinet members can also become the clear point people for advocacy and grassroots organizers, making it easier to unify and amplify ideas and solutions out of favor among those in power. It’s democracy’s insurance policy. And it strengthens the government, too: There is no room for lazy ideas when rivals stand ready. So, what would a shadow cabinet look like here in the United States? We have an amazing wealth of talent on the Democratic side of the aisle in Congress — people loyal to the Constitution and ready to serve in opposition. Democratic leaders Hakeem Jeffries (New York) in the House and Charles E. Schumer (New York) in the Senate could appoint 26 members of Congress to go one-on-one with each member of Trump’s Cabinet. If Trump attempts to weaponize the justice system against his political opponents, we could see Sen. Adam Schiff (California), as our shadow attorney general, arguing against replacing our independent prosecutors with Trump loyalists. If Trump seeks to eliminate the Education Department, Rep. Jahana Hayes (Connecticut), a former teacher of the year, could step up as shadow education secretary to loudly defend it. If Trump tries to hand Ukraine and much of Eastern Europe to Russian President Vladimir Putin, Rep. Gregory W. Meeks (New York), as shadow secretary of state, could be a strong voice in support of maintaining international relationships and protecting democracy at home and abroad. Sen. Michael Bennet (Colorado), as shadow treasury secretary, and Rep. Suzan DelBene (Washington), as shadow commerce secretary, could help ensure the middle class and small businesses have a voice in government, and call out Trump’s proposed tax cuts for billionaires and corporations. Rep. Adam Smith (Washington), as shadow defense secretary, could counter Trump’s threat to use military force against his political opponents and work to ensure that the Defense Department stays loyal to the Constitution and not a wannabe dictator. And Sen. Tammy Duckworth (Illinois) could argue against cuts to the Department of Veterans Affairs that would delay the benefits and care our service members have rightly earned. If Trump hands over the Environmental Protection Agency to the fossil fuel industry, we could see Rep. Kathy Castor (Florida) pushing back for environmental protection and conservation to protect the air we breathe and the water we drink. And if Trump pushes to ban abortion nationwide, Rep. Rosa DeLauro could step in, as shadow secretary of health and human services, to defend women’s right to choose and argue against the gutting of Medicare by Trump and his cronies. New times require new solutions. This shadow cabinet isn’t just about opposing extreme MAGA attacks on our government. It’s also about making clear what we stand for, which is something we didn’t do enough this election cycle. It’s time to show — not just say — what we’re for: justice, accountability and democracy.
---
**HERE'S WHAT'S NEXT:**
North Carolina Democrats have a lot of work ahead of us. We need to organize against the dangerous Trump Administration — from forming a Democratic Shadow Cabinet, to fighting in the courts, to organizing communities on the ground — and we need to get ready to win big in 2026.
**Your continued support is critical as America starts this trying new chapter.[Please pitch in to Wiley's 2026 Fight Fund to be ready for all the work ahead between now and the midterm elections.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96943574/511726196/-1960556440?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241111_PM1_V1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTM3NjZmOS03OGEwLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k_bends9MaPpL-1uUuyxcirIPYAXRdqFGFn7RukAYzI=)**

name: Republican Jewish Coalition
subject: Elise Stefanik = U.N. Ambassador
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Republican Jewish Coalition
body:
We couldn’t be happier with President Trump’s choice.

| |
---
| | | **Peter, President Trump made a fantastic choice in choosing Elise Stefanik to be America 's Ambassador to the United Nations.**

---

body:
thank you, patriot

| [](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=ef0a7757b2084a41a6c83cba4c9628ac&_e=A3Jctsn5QjPzoS6ZKli_j_4VykZrLVmKmOTG1os9Ozx42KzwXOk70IbVOMD74NgDe5zUsuCk9Q9VHnZc_q6OJCTk1ZSEDK0JhBiFWsqthWqoyzn-gmUvqiwsrin7lZACVZndy4O0cfBFHmruXBvhHPPM2dKsAHCbqnoXd7G4gUnL1M3VfSUcnXzi4VAX3B73PfzsBwvWuqHgU5WRI_DwyQfZ6eSyyh4EYaDE_6VsSxBOoPaqXpZq0SyKL0lU5HTuN6tkJhMAMJa3dzmX1K0GopU_2JWms_agRcXVRChCK13HkiwiuMNOe1ow5I2uWlwSl16k2qoYyNxYFGSVsnsyCw%3D%3D)
---
Patriot, **__****_THANK YOU, THANK YOU, THANK YOU, PATRIOT!_** Thanks to all of your dedication and support to Saving America and Keeping North Carolina Great, I'm thrilled to announce that the results are in - and we won. There's simply no way I could've done this without you, and I can't begin to tell you how grateful our family is for your support. It's Americans like you who give me so much hope for the future of our great nation. **It 's time** to get to work in Washington and turn our economy around, secure our border, bring safety back to our streets, and stand up for our First Amendment rights. **It 's time** to make NC-10 the BEST place to live, work, and raise a family in our entire great state. **It 's time** for brand new, battle-tested leadership to represent NC-10 in Washington. **It 's time** to take back our country. Patriot, I'm here to serve YOU - let's get it done. ___Pat Harrigan_
**_P.S. -_**[**_Make sure you 're signed up for Team Harrigan updates here._**](http://links.harriganfornc.com/u/click?_t=687026f8d8f7415aa539c5385c376b1f&_m=ef0a7757b2084a41a6c83cba4c9628ac&_e=A3Jctsn5QjPzoS6ZKli_j_4VykZrLVmKmOTG1os9Ozx42KzwXOk70IbVOMD74NgDe5zUsuCk9Q9VHnZc_q6OJCTk1ZSEDK0JhBiFWsqthWqoyzn-gmUvqiwsrin7lZACVZndy4O0cfBFHmruXBvhHPPM2dKsAHCbqnoXd7G4gUnL1M3VfSUcnXzi4VAX3B73atSnULag1f-6NFvLb_sdF2RplM8yadqnkE_5mYbXDQDRPk4KINQPIVRvVAI8Ctkv6ALZktrJ7Na_fW7q6Qzd8CeWSdvwVWQpiusqYq9n23VWha7ZOdly6jOfG4rYbND3fZWZ8toXi0JXX1wf9GKxuA%3D%3D)**_We 've got a lot of work to do and I want to make sure you don't miss a thing! _**

It 's time to take back our country. Patriot, I'm here to serve YOU - let's get it done. _Pat Harrigan P.S. - 've got a lot of work to do and I want to make sure you don't miss a thing! | Paid for by The Pat Harrigan Committee PO Box 97275, Raleigh, NC 27624 Copyright (C) 2024 Pat Harrigan for Congress, All rights reserved.

name: Ryan Mackenzie
subject: Thank You, Veterans!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mackenzie For Congress]()
body:
| | | | |
---
|
---
| | | | | **Happy Veterans Day!**
I want to thank all our veterans and recognize the sacrifices they have made to keep us free and our nation safe.
**Thank you to all who serve**. Now, it is my honor to work for you in Congress, where I can focus on making sure veterans in our community get the support they need, both during their service and beyond.
This past weekend, I had the opportunity to attend multiple veteran-related events, including the Valor Patriot Ball with Gold Star family Mike and Sally Wargo. I also attended the Veterans Day parade in Carbon County. Everywhere we went the message was clear: **thank you to our veterans**

Trump is the President-Elect and we have brighter days ahead. Electing Trump was no small feat, but Mississippians turned out in record numbers to help elect Donald J. Trump to his second term, in a time of our country that desperately needs his leadership at the helm.
But the Fight is not OVER! With the Republicans taking over the Senate, Mitch McConnell and his spineless Republicans are currently maneuvering behind closed doors to install a new Majority Party leader that will be a roadblock to Trump. There are currently three Senators in the running to lead the Republican Party in the Senate: Senators John Thune (S.D.), John Cornyn (Texas) and Rick Scott (Fla.).
Florida's Senator Rick Scott is the only Senator that has consistently supported Trump and has a proven conservative record to lead the Senate. It has been reported that our MS Senators are supporting Senators Thune and Cornyn. We cannot allow the Swamp to derail the Trump Train!
Don't sit out of the fight now, contact our Mississippi Senators to tell them to vote Senator Rick Scott for Senate Majority Leader!
• Senator Cindy Hyde-Smith at (202) 224-5054
• Senator Roger Wicker at (202) 224-6253

name: Val Hoyle
subject: Veterans Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Val Hoyle for Congress
body:
https://www.valhoyle.com/ [https://www.valhoyle.com/]
Peter,

On Veterans Day, we honor our veterans and service members who have put their lives on the line to serve our nation.

More veterans call Oregon's 4th District home than any other district in our state.

I am honored to work to make sure we stay true to the promises we made to the brave men and women who have served our country. That means making sure they have the good-paying jobs, healthcare, and housing they have earned.

If you or a loved one have served, I would like to thank you for your sacrifice.

— Val
Paid for and authorized by Val Hoyle for Congress.
This email was sent by Val Hoyle [https://www.valhoyle.com/]
Val Hoyle for Congress
PO Box 657
Springfield, OR 97477
United States
Email is an important way for Val Hoyle to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. Too much email? name: Team Jayapal
subject: Here’s how we can start to fight back.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Pramila is hosting a post-election debrief this Wednesday, November 13th at 5:00 pm PT / 8:00 pm ET via Zoom.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20241111-4508-footer&t=1&akid=4508%2E420207%2ED-LJ9q)
Peter, with Donald Trump set to enter the White House in January, Pramila is determined to use every day we have between now and then to build our movement and prepare to fight back.
**As part of that effort, Pramila is hosting a post-election debrief this Wednesday, November 13th at 5:00 pm PT / 8:00 pm ET via Zoom.[Will you sign up to attend now?](https://www.mobilize.us/pramilaforcongress/event/743327/?source=em20241111-4508)**
[](https://www.mobilize.us/pramilaforcongress/event/743327/?source=em20241111-4508)
On the call, Pramila will provide an analysis of the recent election results, discuss what they mean for our nation, and outline the challenges ahead.
This is a crucial opportunity to reflect on the path forward and reaffirm our commitment to protecting democracy and progressive values. And your participation is essential as we continue our work for a stronger, more inclusive future.
[**We have a lot of work ahead of us, Peter, so don’t sit this one out. Sign up for Wednesday’s debrief on Zoom now and let’s get to work to protect our democracy and our values.**](https://www.mobilize.us/pramilaforcongress/event/743327/?source=em20241111-4508)
**[RSVP Now](https://www.mobilize.us/pramilaforcongress/event/743327/?source=em20241111-4508)**
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal

name: Shahid Buttar
subject: How much can change in just five years?
body:
An interview with Rolling Stone on Veterans Day back in 2019 reminds me how far our country has fallen so quickly

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..8hCGvie0LdyxtoUmF9D1CRJ44-TGWDNv6djsUWMI_GU?) for more

# [How much can change in just five years?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151523320&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNTIzMzIwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEzNjY3NDIsImV4cCI6MTczMzk1ODc0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iWCxFMbponTxm00-En3dEPqPJhGIQrHpOb6siMfzDsM)
### An interview with Rolling Stone on Veterans Day back in 2019 reminds me how far our country has fallen so quickly
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Nov 11| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151523320&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNTIzMzIwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMTM2Njc0MiwiZXhwIjoxNzMzOTU4NzQyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sBFUEqkKisup-wjHItrV-D208UpyLC_7v8fTpz5dP-s&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151523320&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNTIzMzIwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEzNjY3NDIsImV4cCI6MTczMzk1ODc0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iWCxFMbponTxm00-En3dEPqPJhGIQrHpOb6siMfzDsM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151523320&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNTIzMzIwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEzNjY3NDIsImV4cCI6MTczMzk1ODc0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iWCxFMbponTxm00-En3dEPqPJhGIQrHpOb6siMfzDsM)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvc2hhaGlkYnV0dGFyL3AvaG93LW11Y2gtY2FuLWNoYW5nZS1pbi1qdXN0LWZpdmU_.MJYv4nBnV7jWI-1e57jYDcDAPy9Ifhw-nfYle8VEwuY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/how-much-can-change-in-just-five?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---
Veterans Day is an apt day to recall not only the sacrifices made by veterans, but also the casual disregard with which Washington [ignores](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b46758-1e84-40d1-b045-f4a9a0187d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their sacrifices, [belittles](https://substack.com/redirect/125453bf-63d8-4a57-9bd9-12b0a0b40bed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their service, and even [insults](https://substack.com/redirect/4313bbc6-f59d-459e-8139-4fcc3b14ee34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their memories even after they have secured military victories.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform and educate your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151523320&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNTIzMzIwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEzNjY3NDIsImV4cCI6MTczMzk1ODc0MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iWCxFMbponTxm00-En3dEPqPJhGIQrHpOb6siMfzDsM)
For me, today's holiday brings to mind a pair of trips to the East Coast. One represented an apex of my work as a politicized musician. The second represented a turning point for my attempts to force political accountability on Washington.
Both feel indescribably poignant to revisit in retrospect. The contrast between then and now is one theme I hope to revisit tomorrow, when I'm excited to [rejoin](https://substack.com/redirect/74e99f3e-815d-4f1d-b90a-2b74986eefe1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) my friends at the _[Redneck Gone Green](https://substack.com/redirect/91c2dcbb-1cee-4411-8eea-3448bd6436c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ podcast](https://substack.com/redirect/91c2dcbb-1cee-4411-8eea-3448bd6436c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at 3pm PT / 6pm ET for a discussion that will be livestreamed on [YouTube](https://substack.com/redirect/ad172623-1a92-4786-923c-a448158bd973?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Rumble](https://substack.com/redirect/13c24fc0-9d77-4f34-aa54-48c8229d8cda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **Mixing a soundtrack for a revolution**
In 2018, I celebrated Veterans Day by playing a DJ set at Catharsis, a regional Burning Man event that happened on the national mall, directly across the street from Trump's White House. I had written about [the first installment of Catharsis](https://substack.com/redirect/14a5ed25-67e2-4881-94ec-6ea2ef13e15e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the Burning Man Journal, and was grateful to play a role despite having moved from DC back to San Francisco in 2015 in order to join the staff of the Electronic Frontier Foundation.
My music usually features political themes, but my set the weekend of Veterans Day in 2018 might have been the most overtly political one I'd played to that point. Featuring the voices of Bernie Sanders, Greta Thunberg, and Malcolm X, it felt like the soundtrack for a revolution that I tried to escalate in the following years by running for Congress against one of the most powerful figures in Washington.
I was not in a position to record the set, but a fan thankfully recorded one of my live rhymes from the dance floor.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0e6d29f0-5625-40e8-b21f-79719c4546ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### **Taking a fight across the country **
In 2019, Veterans Day saw me back on the east coast, this time in New York for media interviews related to my campaign for Congress. That day was [my first appearance on ](https://substack.com/redirect/f9cb8232-53a5-4bbf-bc08-987c15463adb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Rolling Stone](https://substack.com/redirect/f9cb8232-53a5-4bbf-bc08-987c15463adb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ with Katie Halper and Matt Taibbi, and the discussion represented one among many inflection points as my efforts gained greater attention around the country. It exposed my voice to a national audience, attracted an influx of resources to my campaign, and included a number of themes that felt bizarrely prophetic in the light of later events.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work exposing bipartisan corruption, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..2Zq82BBB-b0GqkAD1xF4qSXinNiVZVTzFVP23GAkHW4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=151523320)
While the interview is behind a paywall [on the Rolling Stone website](https://substack.com/redirect/f9cb8232-53a5-4bbf-bc08-987c15463adb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), it remains publicly available on YouTube.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c23790b2-4e8c-4ba5-9fef-7c3725089dc7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
### **I really wish I were wrong more often**
Several parts of this interview struck me as mildly heartbreaking to revisit. It pains me to consider how much could be so different today had our country heeded the counsel of voices like mine when we had the chance.
Discussing some of the many areas where Democrats base opportunistic opinions on confused impressions of the electorate, I [observed](https://substack.com/redirect/16ac15ef-92ee-442c-914a-9ffa28f64df3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "We don't live in Raegan's America anymore."
The continuing impression among Democratic Party strategists that we do is one big reason why Kamala Harris lost last week's election. Rather than articulate principles and policies responding to the needs of struggling families, the Harris campaign [chose](https://substack.com/redirect/eaa2c0c5-12a7-4f1d-8777-b73cc238fe1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to highlight her endorsements by voices who were once establishment Republicans, like Liz Cheney.
Matt reflected five years ago on the same pattern, [noting](https://substack.com/redirect/bd2ec17c-f979-4d21-adb4-7984efe248b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) how "It's almost like Democrats want to run the same 2016 strategy, just talking about Donald Trump." Sound familiar?
Electoral strategy was only one area in which our discussion illuminated the blind spots of today's Democratic Party. We also discussed the impeachment of President Trump, for which I had [advocated](https://substack.com/redirect/6a0e1be2-a7e5-4aca-8fd7-11e41127d702?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) long before Pelosi showed up like a boxer throwing a fight.
Katie & Matt asked me about my argument for impeachment--which was shared by Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD). He led the impeachment process in the House and recommended the inclusion of charges based on Trump's violations of the Emoluments Clause, only to be rejected by then House Speaker Nancy Pelosi. As I [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/6fa9962e-f41e-4b39-87ae-012c1502b1db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the interview, charges based on the Emoluments Clause would:
> flip votes in the Senate. There is nothing that enrages the conservative base more than the idea of their taxes being misused. And if the impeachment proceeding was...an explanation for the American people of all the different ways this criminal is stealing from you, that will flip Republican votes in the Senate, but a contrived, limited process that turns a blind eye to corruption...is set up to fail."
I offered the recipe for how to save our Republic from the machinations of a potential tyrant, but it went ignored.
### **Generational change is inexorable**
The theme of generational change echoed across the interview. At a [later point](https://substack.com/redirect/10c5e07e-ef38-43c7-93c8-6c17369b1727?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I describe it as "inexorable."
> The centrists can't stop the future. People my age recognize the moral bankruptcy of capitalism, drug war, and wars for profit....Human rights should take primacy over corporate profit...peace should take primacy over war profiteering. The American people recognize principle when they hear it.
My dedication to principles (rather than politics) came up again [later](https://substack.com/redirect/6384c084-46d6-485c-a7b4-74e273e04c9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> That's one reason I describe myself as a transpartisan constitutionalist. I am a Democratic Socialist, and I am eager to establish healthcare, and housing, and food as human rights--and, at the same time, I recognize that there are all these principles that bring together the American people regardless of our ideology and...partisan stripes. That's the opportunity of this moment: to speak to that community of Americans who are willing to see through the contrived dysfunction in Washington.
This was also one of my only interviews meaningfully exploring my work helping [establish LGBTQ rights](https://substack.com/redirect/7070960a-d8f2-475f-9992-d444e2442920?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It felt "poignant for me to be in NY State, where I had a chance to organize the very first litigation vindicating the right of consenting adults to marry a partner of their choice. A lot of progressives and liberals will recognize marriage equality as one of the few issues that we've actually gained ground on over the last generation, and I'm very proud to have been an early advocate for it."
### **Democracy matters --but it means more than Democrats think**
At [one point](https://substack.com/redirect/077e617a-67e9-4b1d-91d9-30ca8f795bb8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), co-host Matt Taibbi reflected on my roots in the movement to protect government transparency and freedoms of conscience and association from pervasive government surveillance and secrecy.
His question led me to reflect on how "the people we describe as liberals today don't deserve the word. I'm a socialist because I am very proudly committed to a set of ideals that liberals are unwilling to claim, but they won't even claim their _own_ ideals...They're authoritarians." That exchange led to a further discussion exploring [constitutional theory](https://substack.com/redirect/f326688d-44f6-4621-a317-1def3bb11609?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the full meaning of the First Amendment beyond merely a right to expression, before we later [returned](https://substack.com/redirect/2fbc9c79-59be-4f26-810d-b243fc37fffa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the theme of "the triumph of identity over ideas in the Democratic Party."
Later, co-host Katie Halper asked about [my 2015 arrest](https://substack.com/redirect/8604eb2f-0be8-4b74-97b7-60db3250fb27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. Senate, which offered a chance to reflect on some dynamics that others have come to recognize only more recently. I explained:
> "We have lethal 'justice' without a charge or a trial for powerless people, and we have entirely permissive 'justice' that awards public pensions to people who commit grave crimes against the Republic and then lie about them--under a Democratic presidency. That was before Trump...
> Democrats have been entirely complicit in the erosion of democracy that preceded this criminal President. That's one of the reasons why I think the party doesn't deserve its label. The authoritarian party might fit, or the Fecklessness party, the Weak Party, the Co-opted Party...Pick one."
The co-hosts offered some memorable responses. Matt suggested that "It's kind of both. It's both authoritarian and feckless." Katie followed up by [observing](https://substack.com/redirect/b142836a-9303-4028-aab7-8739a38757d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) how, "It's not that Democrats are inept (they are, in some ways) or spineless (though they are), but they want these things. They need to _pretend_ that they're opposing them."
### **How far we 've fallen**
More than any other theme, what pains me the most about this interview is seeing how far our national policy discourse has swung to the right in the years since I was silenced. That election cycle offered a seeming opportunity to make real progress.
Back in 2019, I [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/3776520d-ec0e-4091-9566-f7ddfeca0910?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "the American people recognize that our entire political system is up to its neck in corporate money. It's why we don't have Medicare for All, it's why people die every day because they can't get access to the preventive care that they need. It's why we're racing humanity and the Earth--the entire Biosphere--off a climate cliff."
Later in the interview, I stepped back to [observe](https://substack.com/redirect/1544fd8e-051e-4eef-bec2-91a929718c27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that "You couldn't design policies that are more hostile to human beings if you tried. In the climate context, in the foreign policy context, in the healthcare context, and in all of these places, I see capitalism--that is, the rule by capital over people--demonstrated. I would much rather see people control capital than capital control people."
Rather than merely hold the line, the movement for a sane future has dramatically receded since then. When's the last time we heard anyone advocating for a right to basic human needs?
If you come across other voices articulating principles like these, please share them with me. I'd like to highlight them where I can.
_Paid subscribers can access a further reflection on one reason I 'm excited by, and grateful for, the results of last week's elections..._

clean_body: An interview with Rolling Stone on Veterans Day back in 2019 reminds me how far our country has fallen so quickly | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? An interview with Rolling Stone on Veterans Day back in 2019 reminds me how far our country has fallen so quickly | | | Nov 11| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Veterans Day is an apt day to recall not only the sacrifices made by veterans, but also the casual disregard with which Washington their sacrifices, their service, and even their memories even after they have secured military victories. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform and educate your friends by sharing this post. For me, today's holiday brings to mind a pair of trips to the East Coast. One represented an apex of my work as a politicized musician. The second represented a turning point for my attempts to force political accountability on Washington. Both feel indescribably poignant to revisit in retrospect. The contrast between then and now is one theme I hope to revisit tomorrow, when I'm excited to my friends at the at 3pm PT / 6pm ET for a discussion that will be livestreamed on and . Mixing a soundtrack for a revolution In 2018, I celebrated Veterans Day by playing a DJ set at Catharsis, a regional Burning Man event that happened on the national mall, directly across the street from Trump's White House. I had written about for the Burning Man Journal, and was grateful to play a role despite having moved from DC back to San Francisco in 2015 in order to join the staff of the Electronic Frontier Foundation. My music usually features political themes, but my set the weekend of Veterans Day in 2018 might have been the most overtly political one I'd played to that point. Featuring the voices of Bernie Sanders, Greta Thunberg, and Malcolm X, it felt like the soundtrack for a revolution that I tried to escalate in the following years by running for Congress against one of the most powerful figures in Washington. I was not in a position to record the set, but a fan thankfully recorded one of my live rhymes from the dance floor. Taking a fight across the country In 2019, Veterans Day saw me back on the east coast, this time in New York for media interviews related to my campaign for Congress. That day was with Katie Halper and Matt Taibbi, and the discussion represented one among many inflection points as my efforts gained greater attention around the country. It exposed my voice to a national audience, attracted an influx of resources to my campaign, and included a number of themes that felt bizarrely prophetic in the light of later events. Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription! To support my work exposing bipartisan corruption, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. While the interview is behind a paywall , it remains publicly available on YouTube. I really wish I were wrong more often Several parts of this interview struck me as mildly heartbreaking to revisit. It pains me to consider how much could be so different today had our country heeded the counsel of voices like mine when we had the chance. Discussing some of the many areas where Democrats base opportunistic opinions on confused impressions of the electorate, I that "We don't live in Raegan's America anymore." The continuing impression among Democratic Party strategists that we do is one big reason why Kamala Harris lost last week's election. Rather than articulate principles and policies responding to the needs of struggling families, the Harris campaign to highlight her endorsements by voices who were once establishment Republicans, like Liz Cheney. Matt reflected five years ago on the same pattern, how "It's almost like Democrats want to run the same 2016 strategy, just talking about Donald Trump." Sound familiar? Electoral strategy was only one area in which our discussion illuminated the blind spots of today's Democratic Party. We also discussed the impeachment of President Trump, for which I had long before Pelosi showed up like a boxer throwing a fight. Katie & Matt asked me about my argument for impeachment--which was shared by Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD). He led the impeachment process in the House and recommended the inclusion of charges based on Trump's violations of the Emoluments Clause, only to be rejected by then House Speaker Nancy Pelosi. As I in the interview, charges based on the Emoluments Clause would: flip votes in the Senate. There is nothing that enrages the conservative base more than the idea of their taxes being misused. And if the impeachment proceeding was...an explanation for the American people of all the different ways this criminal is stealing from you, that will flip Republican votes in the Senate, but a contrived, limited process that turns a blind eye to corruption...is set up to fail." I offered the recipe for how to save our Republic from the machinations of a potential tyrant, but it went ignored. Generational change is inexorable The theme of generational change echoed across the interview. At a , I describe it as "inexorable." The centrists can't stop the future. People my age recognize the moral bankruptcy of capitalism, drug war, and wars for profit....Human rights should take primacy over corporate profit...peace should take primacy over war profiteering. The American people recognize principle when they hear it. My dedication to principles (rather than politics) came up again . That's one reason I describe myself as a transpartisan constitutionalist. I am a Democratic Socialist, and I am eager to establish healthcare, and housing, and food as human rights--and, at the same time, I recognize that there are all these principles that bring together the American people regardless of our ideology and...partisan stripes. That's the opportunity of this moment: to speak to that community of Americans who are willing to see through the contrived dysfunction in Washington. This was also one of my only interviews meaningfully exploring my work helping . It felt "poignant for me to be in NY State, where I had a chance to organize the very first litigation vindicating the right of consenting adults to marry a partner of their choice. A lot of progressives and liberals will recognize marriage equality as one of the few issues that we've actually gained ground on over the last generation, and I'm very proud to have been an early advocate for it." Democracy matters --but it means more than Democrats think At , co-host Matt Taibbi reflected on my roots in the movement to protect government transparency and freedoms of conscience and association from pervasive government surveillance and secrecy. His question led me to reflect on how "the people we describe as liberals today don't deserve the word. I'm a socialist because I am very proudly committed to a set of ideals that liberals are unwilling to claim, but they won't even claim their own ideals...They're authoritarians." That exchange led to a further discussion exploring and the full meaning of the First Amendment beyond merely a right to expression, before we later to the theme of "the triumph of identity over ideas in the Democratic Party." Later, co-host Katie Halper asked about in the U.S. Senate, which offered a chance to reflect on some dynamics that others have come to recognize only more recently. I explained: "We have lethal 'justice' without a charge or a trial for powerless people, and we have entirely permissive 'justice' that awards public pensions to people who commit grave crimes against the Republic and then lie about them--under a Democratic presidency. That was before Trump... Democrats have been entirely complicit in the erosion of democracy that preceded this criminal President. That's one of the reasons why I think the party doesn't deserve its label. The authoritarian party might fit, or the Fecklessness party, the Weak Party, the Co-opted Party...Pick one." The co-hosts offered some memorable responses. Matt suggested that "It's kind of both. It's both authoritarian and feckless." Katie followed up by how, "It's not that Democrats are inept (they are, in some ways) or spineless (though they are), but they want these things. They need to pretend that they're opposing them." How far we 've fallen More than any other theme, what pains me the most about this interview is seeing how far our national policy discourse has swung to the right in the years since I was silenced. That election cycle offered a seeming opportunity to make real progress. Back in 2019, I that "the American people recognize that our entire political system is up to its neck in corporate money. It's why we don't have Medicare for All, it's why people die every day because they can't get access to the preventive care that they need. It's why we're racing humanity and the Earth--the entire Biosphere--off a climate cliff." Later in the interview, I stepped back to that "You couldn't design policies that are more hostile to human beings if you tried. In the climate context, in the foreign policy context, in the healthcare context, and in all of these places, I see capitalism--that is, the rule by capital over people--demonstrated. I would much rather see people control capital than capital control people." Rather than merely hold the line, the movement for a sane future has dramatically receded since then. When's the last time we heard anyone advocating for a right to basic human needs? If you come across other voices articulating principles like these, please share them with me. I'd like to highlight them where I can. Paid subscribers can access a further reflection on one reason I 'm excited by, and grateful for, the results of last week's elections... Continue reading this post for free in the Substack app | | | | | | | (C) 2024 Shahid Buttar South Lake Tahoe, CA 96151 ? ?

But our work is far from over. As we celebrate this triumph, we must acknowledge the looming threats from the radical left. They'll stop at nothing to undermine our values and dismantle the progress we've made. As reported, Demcrats are already working to undermine President Trump’s mandate — with California Governor Gavin Newsom making plans to “Trump-proof” his liberal state. We can't let them succeed.
